programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the town to get to the allotment to pick some vegetables. On the way they cheer up a sad lamp post, count loaves of bread in the baker’s window, jump in muddy puddles and even find a golden chicken!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Simon and Evie take Waffle for a walk and bump into Evie's friend Anaya and her mum, AJ. An overenthusiastic Waffle drags Simon through the park, soaking him under a sprinkler

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly decides to make a film with Alice to cheer her up, but all sorts of things go wrong and then Mack manages to erase the film completely. How can Molly save the day?

programme.title: Topsy and Tim
programme.desc: When Topsy and Tim's friends Rony and Vinda come to play, things do not quite go to plan at first - but no one wants to go home by the time their mums arrive

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Hannah's family is from South Korea, and she will be making a Korean salmon marinade, which is a popular recipe used there. She starts by adding garlic puree, Korean chilli paste, soy sauce, honey, toasted sesame oil and her special ingredient, sesame seeds, into a jug and mixing them all up. Next, Hannah puts pieces of her salmon onto individual baking sheets and spoons the marinade on. She garnishes them with freshly chopped spring onions, and then her salmon is ready to be cooked in the oven. Hannah wears a colourful Korean outfit and treats her friends to some local Korean kimchi, but will her pals be able to guess her special ingredient?

programme.title: Apple Tree House
programme.desc: When Sam, David and Kobi find a lost piano, they aim to reunite it with the owner

programme.title: Old Jack's Boat
programme.desc: Jack and Salty visit the Humbug Hotel and help a lonely tigerfish find true love

programme.title: Down on the Farm
programme.desc: A look at what happens on farms - from livestock to harvesting as well as where food comes from and how it is grown. Presented by ex-JLS member and farmer JB Gill, and Storm Huntley

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the Squirrels meet a lion with unfortunate breath

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Animated preschool series about a clubhouse that is run by a big dog called Duggee

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The Go Jetters join Ubercorn in the Go Jet Academy to try out a brand new super vacuum that reportedly can suck up bad weather

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: When Grim HQ's heating gets stuck on, Glitch looks for a cold place to land and cool down. Meanwhile, the Go Jetters enjoy the cool sights of Harbin Ice City in China. Every year sculptors at the festival create realistic models of world famous landmarks from blocks of ice and Lars is attempting to carve the Eiffel Tower

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bob is stuck in a runaway hot air balloon and needs clear instructions to land it

programme.title: B.O.T and the Beasties
programme.desc: B.O.T. is in Spacey Glowy World. The Glowy Shiner is easily frightened and keeps dazzling B.O.T. with its bright tail. B.O.T. stops chasing after the shy beastie and, by being calm and still, manages to collect the data and complete the mission.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie visits a hospital to get her hearing tested and finds out how whistles are made

programme.title: What's On Your Head?
programme.desc: Richard, who is Jewish, shows us his Kippah and tells us why he decided to wear one.

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Harriet helps Timmy find a painted egg in a treasure hunt

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo is excited to show Gran Gran around the city farm - it's been a few months since she was last there, and she can't wait to see Melanie the chick again. But when they arrive at the chicken enclosure, they can't find the little fluffy chick - where could she be?

programme.title: CBeebies Presents
programme.desc: William Shakespeare is back on CBeebies with an adaptation of one of his most famous plays, Romeo and Juliet. With the help of a host of CBeebies' stars and actors, Rebecca the Librarian and William Shakespeare put on a performance of the play in a magical library. The story twists and turns as the Montague and Capulet families squabble, and Romeo and Juliet meet and fall in love. There are songs and dances to join in with as the greatest love story ever told comes to life in CBeebies' latest stage show performance.

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: This episode follows Liberty as she celebrates the Christian holiday of Easter with her family. Liberty and her family begin by visiting a farm, where they see spring lambs and learn all about Easter. During their time at the farm, they all go on an epic scavenger hunt where they collect as many eggs as possible. As Liberty has so much fun on the scavenger hunt, she suggests that they have their own easter egg hunt once they get home. Once Liberty and her family have found all of the chocolate eggs, they decide to melt them down and make tasty chocolate flake cakes. After lots of Easter fun, they go to church for a huge celebration. Liberty and her family finish the day with a lovely picnic and an egg rolling competition.

programme.title: The Toddler Club at Home
programme.desc: Ideas of activities to do with your toddler that are all about exploring words. See how spotting words with your toddler and introducing them to new words helps them develop their language skills. Hosted by Giovanna from her own home.

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Treasure Champs
programme.desc: Kari has been training hard for a dance competition, but Barry has not, meaning he is not ready to become the perfect dance partner she needs to win the contest|

programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects associated with the colour orange, from oranges to orangutans, and explore the deeper meaning of the colour. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour orange.

programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie on an adventure around the world of flowers to see how we can help to look after them and our planet. Find out how flowers and different animals, like bees, birds, butterflies and insects, work together. We learn how bees and insects pollinate flowers, as Maddie becomes a bee and plays the pollination game! Along the way, we’ll discover flowers of all different shapes, sizes and colours and learn why they are so different. We discover the different parts of a flower, the stigma, the anther, the stamen and the petals and how some flowers go to sleep at night and open in the day!

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Ann Teak and Professor Muddles join team captains Keith Fitt and Gail Force for funny clips and questions, but today Arthur is having a problem with the plumbing and the studio is filled with leaky old pipes. In spite of this, the show kicks off and Round 1 is a simple clip game with a question for each team. Keith and Ann Teak are up first. Arthur presents a messy clip starring Captain Adorable and his custard pie button, but can the team remember how many custard pies splatted out of the wall? Next up, Gail and Professor Muddles are presented with a clip of DIY Dan, who has lost his mobile phone inside a pile of removal boxes, but can they remember what ringing sound his mobile phone was making? All this talk of phones reminds Arthur that he needs to ring a plumber to fix the pipes, but before Arthur can even make the call, Keith dumps his toolbox down on the desk and insists that he knows how to fix the pipes himself! Round 2 is next, and it’s time for Nana’s Panto Showdown. This ti

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie borrows some wellies from her grandmother that once belonged to a girl called Izzy, and decides to try to find this mysterious person

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: After a night of strong rain, the Shallots set sail on a seafaring adventure

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: A disappointed Lola learns she has been cast as a leaf instead of the sun in the school play

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos ride in the Pinky Ponk, and have fun guessing who will be the next person to climb on board

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: A tale is read for younger viewers before they go to bed

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Heslington, a village near York, where he revisits a derelict building where the new owner had some grand plans. Dion Dublin visits Wolverhampton to see a charming bungalow, and Martel Maxwell is in Cardiff to see a first-floor flat

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the brutal attack of a 96-year-old D-Day war veteran on his doorstep. Police in Taunton, Somerset, quickly arrested his attacker after tracking his spending habits

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics Dan and Leigh are called to assist bomb disposal experts after a hazardous chemical is found at a building site. Lee and Nadine race to the scene of a three-vehicle smash on icy roads in Melrose, while paramedics in Oban make two trips to visit a regular patient whose breathing is deteriorating

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique hunting with two teams at the Royal Cheshire Showground. Presenter Eric Knowles observes proceedings and also discovers the story of two extraordinary Victorian women who brought ancient Egypt to Macclesfield

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth struggles to protect those closest to him when an old foe is unexpectedly released from prison. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Pembrokeshire with a couple who want not only to buy their first house together, but also need accommodation for holidaymakers and a horse

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter and garden designer Arit Anderson visit gardens of reflection and contemplation. At the remote Pluscarden Abbey in north-east Scotland, Alexander helps harvest luscious apples and accompanies the monks singing midday psalms. Meanwhile, Arit walks in the footsteps of royalty in the glorious grounds of Sudeley Castle. Plus, Alexander returns to his student haunts in Cambridge to discover a place designed to inspire joy, even on the bleakest days, in the university's Botanic Garden

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A beloved stoneware elephant's fall from a mantlepiece left its sentimental owner shattered into tiny pieces along with the ornament, so ceramics expert Kirsten Ramsay sets about performing a delicate restoration to revive both Michel's precious item and her memories. Plus, Will Kirk and Suzie Fletcher join forces to resurrect a dilapidated child's rocking horse and Brenton West undertakes the repair of an unusual trophy shaped like a boxing ring

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon heads to the gym with the estate agent, where Ben, keen to scupper her plans, makes his own offer on the business. Lily asks Jean if she and the other kids can have an afternoon tea party - but when their gran goes off to buy supplies, the scheming youngster packs a suitcase. Callum's wedding plans are causing problems and embarrassment

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last challenge of the semi-finals sees the six remaining amateurs cook-off to win a coveted place in the MasterChef Finals. They are tasked to each cook an outstanding dish to impress revered restaurant critics Grace Dent, Jay Rayner and Jimi Famurewa. Serving food to these masters of the dining room is a daunting task for the most adept of chefs, and these talented home-cooks now have to put their skills and palates on the line. Any mistake could cost them the competition, so the pressure is firmly on. It remains to be seen who John Torode and Gregg Wallace will take through to the prestigious finals week

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Filmed before the coronavirus crisis swept the nation, the comedian takes on one of his toughest challenges yet, working as a carer. As well as facing the challenge of doing intimate personal care, Rhod is nearly drowned by an octogenarian in a swimming pool, and finds himself upstaged for bawdy humour by a poet with Parkinsons

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Octavia Spencer and Melissa McCarthy, who discuss working together in superhero comedy Thunder Force, while David Schwimmer and Nick Mohammed - co-stars of spy series Intelligence - also appear. Comedian Frank Skinner and singer and star of RuPaul's Drag Race Michelle Visage are also on Graham's guest roster. Plus, Steps perform Heartbreak in This City

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce takes on the challenge of giving up sex for a month, visiting a psychologist to understand her own desires and investigating the pressures young people experience in their sex lives owing to online porn. She meets a celibate rapper who intends to wait until his wedding night and gets some tips on finding alternative ways to connect with her partner

programme.title: Spooks: The Greater Good (2015)
programme.desc: A terrorist escapes custody during a prisoner handover when the convoy he is being transported in is attacked, leading to a CIA operative being killed and relations becoming frayed between British and American intelligence agencies. MI5 officer Will Holloway must team up with disgraced intelligence chief Harry Pearce to track him down before an imminent terrorist attack on London. Spy thriller, starring Kit Harington and Peter Firth

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Shivi Ramoutar, plus special guest Mel Giedroyc. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick wine to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Four friends take on a terraced house in Crewe. A former shop in a Cumbrian village and a three-bedroom terraced house in Gnosall in Staffordshire are also refurbished

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's top-flight fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City and Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A mother and son take on a mother and daughter at the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron help the teams while Anita Manning leads proceedings

programme.title: Ready Steady Cook
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal oversees the action as sisters Elsa and Gabriella pair up with chefs Jeremy Pang and Akis Petretzikis to prepare a meal in 20 minutes

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who have £500,000 to spend on a large family home in Northern Ireland where they can also keep animals and welcome paying guests. Away from the search, Nicki immerses herself in the country's maritime history at the dry dock where RMS Titanic was built

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Anu and Ash live in Coventry with their cat Quinn, where their garden is currently a new build blank canvas divided into two cramped tiers. The couple want to turn it into a pet-friendly delight, inspired by the gardens and countryside they fell in love with on holidays in Tuscany, Italy. They want lots of interest for Quinn and a large inviting patio where they can entertain their friends and family. Arit Anderson and Charlie Dimmock have a £3,500 budget to make the couple's dreams come true

programme.title: Wales: Land of the Wild
programme.desc: An epic journey following Wales's rivers, from their birth in the mountains to the sea, discovering the hidden secrets of wildlife. Super slow-motion cameras film peregrine falcons on the hunt and reveal why their prey, the unsuspecting red grouse, are often able to evade the fastest predator on Earth. Dragonflies' amazing aerial acrobatics are slowed down 400 times their normal speed and red squirrels are captured as they leap through the trees. In a city park, pigeons hidden superpowers are laid bare, and in an upland garden a subtle hierarchy is at play as birds gather around a feeder

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites Steve Backshall, Noreen Khan, Rhys James and Caroline Flint to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are sharks, Marilyn Monroe, The US remake of The Office and classic horror movie Alien. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A married couple from Merthyr Tydfil answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz featuring teams of comedians. Rosie Jones, Miles Jupp, Russell Kane, Lucy Beaumont, Rhys James, Lloyd Griffith, Phil Wang and Sindhu Vee try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie nearly chokes to death while on a walk in the woods with Grace, forcing her to perform a makeshift surgical procedure using what she has to hand to save his life - an action that could have devastating consequences for both him and her relationship with Connie. Rash discovers that his dad has been lying about his relationship, and Jade sees past her suspicions of Tina

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose comes back into Faith's life for the first time in 25 years, claiming to want to clear the air before she dies. However, Faith is not sure she can believe or trust her. Osian's case has its first hearing in court, and Judge Alwen Owens agrees to meet up with him to try to understand what he wants. Steve is racked with guilt over his failure to provide financial stability for his children, and Evan begins spying on Faith

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City and Chelsea v West Bromwich Albion. The Foxes and the Blues were looking for three points to boost their chances of finishing in the top four, but Leicester faced the toughest possible test against the champions elect. Plus, Arsenal v Liverpool and Leeds United v Sheffield United

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the top 10 Premier League hard men, featuring the likes of Roy Keane, Vinnie Jones, Patrick Vieira and Duncan Ferguson

programme.title: Bobby (2016)
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating Bobby Moore, exploring his football career, including leading the England team to victory in the 1966 world cup, and the personal challenges he faced both on the pitch and in retirement. Featuring archive footage and contributions from family, friends and celebrity fans

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City and Chelsea v West Bromwich Albion. The Foxes and the Blues were looking for three points to boost their chances of finishing in the top four, but Leicester faced the toughest possible test against the champions elect. Plus, Arsenal v Liverpool and Leeds United v Sheffield United

programme.title: Fern Britton's Holy Land Journey
programme.desc: The presenter travels to Jerusalem on a life-changing journey of faith, in search of the real story of Jesus' final days, from his triumphal arrival, to his brutal crucifixion

programme.title: Easter Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Most Rev Justin Welby shares his Easter message of hope and resurrection, in a joyful celebration, live from the stunning setting of Canterbury Cathedral. Following Covid-safe guidelines, members of Canterbury Cathedral Choir, directed by David Newsholme, sing well-loved hymns and anthems including Jesus Christ is Risen Today, This Joyful Eastertide and Thine be the Glory. The Mass setting is Mozart's Missa Brevis in B Flat. With Gospel music from Junior Garr and members of the Spirituals Choir and readings and prayers from the Rt Rev Dr Rose Hudson-Wilkin, Bishop of Dover, and members of the Community of St Anselm

programme.title: Urbi et Orbi
programme.desc: Pope Francis gives his Easter Message and Blessing on the ninth Easter of his pontificate

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: Part two of two. Alexander and Arit Anderson visit gardens expressing hope, joy and renewal. At the Bishop's Palace in Wells, Somerset, Alexander discovers why this place has been sacred for centuries, and helps to create a colourful and uplifting work of art. Arit learns how the ancient trees at Scone Palace in Perthshire will be given a new lease of life, and Alexander heads to his home county of Northumberland to tour a garden using humour to plant seeds of change in the community

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and David Harper help two teams buy items at Southwell Racecourse in Nottinghamshire, while presenter Eric Knowles visits the Museum of Timekeeping in Newark

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins celebrates Easter Sunday from Llandaff Cathedral in Cardiff. She finds out about the meaning behind the Paschal Candle, while James Lusted meets a vicar who has turned his life around. Featuring special performances from Bryn Terfel, Llandaff Cathedral Choir, and Vocal Creation

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of teachers with a £600,000 budget who want to upsize and find the ideal country home for them and their four young children. Away from property, Jonnie turns the heat up on his Lancastrian tour when he visits one of the last working forges in the county

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: The Boat Race
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the men's and women's University Boat Races between Oxford and Cambridge, relocated this year to the River Great Ouse in Cambridgeshire. The race was cancelled last year and it was Cambridge who prevailed in both races in 2019 at the traditional venue of the River Thames, with two-time Olympic champion James Cracknell among the members of their men's team, becoming the oldest competitor in the event's history at the age of 46. With analysis from Cracknell, Matthew Pinsent and Katherine Grainger, and commentary by Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents the show from the Golden Triangle in Gloucestershire, an area famous for wild daffodils. She talks to local people who remember picking the wild flowers as children and visits some of the finest medieval wall paintings in England. Charlotte Smith investigates the challenges facing rural churches and the communities they serve, and a mother and son reveal how they have fulfilled their dreams of farming in the Cumbrian hills

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Culzean Castle in South West Scotland, and uncovers the story of a former slave who established himself at the heart of the local community. Treasures brought in to be valued include a 1914 FA cup medal, a valuable Art Nouveau clock, and a huge statue found in a compost heap

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness confront the male mid-life crisis by buying the perfect sports cars for a middle-aged gentleman. Back at the track, Chris tests out the raucous new Toyota GR Yaris hot hatch

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: On the strength of evidence from a new witness, Jo brings Terry Boyle back in for questioning about Gail Vella's murder. Kate is torn between loyalty to Jo and wanting to assist AC-12's investigation - and tensions only increase when she encounters a face from the past with links to organised crime

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Newcastle United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion. Spurs and the Red Devils were looking to boost their chances of qualifying for the Champions League with victories against sides battling against relegation. Plus, Southampton v Burnley, Aston Villa v Fulham and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the latest Super League fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City and Chelsea v Birmingham City

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts the property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house, while a celebrity panel try work out which one is telling the truth. This week, regulars Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix are joined by Rob Rinder to find the real Michael. From the first sight of the four players the panel are instantly thrown by a familiar face, while the path to the truth is obscured by another Michael's inability to remember anything about his own life

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A married couple from Merthyr Tydfil answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

Callum's wedding plans are causing problems and embarrassment

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Lacking other options, Caitlin becomes her own lawyer and as she asks Maggie questions about Joe's death, frustrations boil over. Paul's test to check if he's a match for Rory makes Lenny unexpectedly protective, and Sonny's chat with Scarlett about Stevie brings out the worst in her

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last challenge of the semi-finals sees the six remaining amateurs cook-off to win a coveted place in the MasterChef Finals. programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Octavia Spencer and Melissa McCarthy, who discuss working together in superhero comedy Thunder Force, while David Schwimmer and Nick Mohammed - co-stars of spy series Intelligence - also appear. Comedian Frank Skinner and singer and star of RuPaul's Drag Race Michelle Visage are also on Graham's guest roster. Plus, Steps perform Heartbreak in This City

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents a special edition looking back over the best moments from the series. The celebrity panellists joining team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell include Jo Brand, Daisy May Cooper, Stacey Dooley, Ronan Keating, Prue Leith, Scarlett Moffatt, Diane Morgan and Bob Mortimer

programme.title: A View from the Terrace
programme.desc: Craig G Telfer invites contributors Joel Sked, Shaughan McGuigan and Craig Fowler to dissect the ins and outs of the Scottish footballing week just passed, offering analysis and opinion in their own unique way. They are joined by social media's Robert Borthwick, a man known for his wit and whimsy on Twitter that riles and rallies fans of every club in equal measure

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Gennaro Contaldo and Shivi Ramoutar, plus special guest Mel Giedroyc. Olly Smith joins via video link to pick wine to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Four friends take on a terraced house in Crewe. A former shop in a Cumbrian village and a three-bedroom terraced house in Gnosall in Staffordshire are also refurbished

programme.title: Sportscene Live
programme.desc: Dumbarton v Aberdeen (Kick-off 12.15pm). All the action from the third-round tie at C&G Systems Stadium. League One outfit Dumbarton recorded a convincing 4-0 victory over Huntly here in the previous round, but the odds are stacked against them as they face the Dons, who will expect to move a step closer to lifting the trophy for the first time since 1990

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a couple who have £500,000 to spend on a large family home in Northern Ireland where they can also keep animals and welcome paying guests. Away from the search, Nicki immerses herself in the country's maritime history at the dry dock where RMS Titanic was built

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team transforms a filing cabinet, petrol can, table and chest of drawers from tips in Greater Manchester and Surrey in the hope of making a profit

programme.title: Sportscene Results
programme.desc: David Currie presents the classified results from the third round of the Scottish Cup and a round-up of the afternoon's main football news. Plus, a look back at the week's talking points

programme.title: Celebrity Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites Steve Backshall, Noreen Khan, Rhys James and Caroline Flint to take to the black chair to be put to the test on their chosen specialist subjects, which are sharks, Marilyn Monroe, The US remake of The Office and classic horror movie Alien. The second round sees them tackling general knowledge questions in a bid to carry off the commemorative trophy

programme.title: Catchpoint
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness hosts a new family-friendly game show combining physical fun and an exciting question mechanic, as contestants have to think fast as they answer questions while trying to catch balls of various sizes dropped from the studio ceiling, with a variety of entertaining rounds and a cash prize at stake

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A married couple from Merthyr Tydfil answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special edition of the quiz featuring teams of comedians. Rosie Jones, Miles Jupp, Russell Kane, Lucy Beaumont, Rhys James, Lloyd Griffith, Phil Wang and Sindhu Vee try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Ollie nearly chokes to death while on a walk in the woods with Grace, forcing her to perform a makeshift surgical procedure using what she has to hand to save his life - an action that could have devastating consequences for both him and her relationship with Connie. Rash discovers that his dad has been lying about his relationship, and Jade sees past her suspicions of Tina

programme.title: Keeping Faith
programme.desc: Rose comes back into Faith's life for the first time in 25 years, claiming to want to clear the air before she dies. However, Faith is not sure she can believe or trust her. Osian's case has its first hearing in court, and Judge Alwen Owens agrees to meet up with him to try to understand what he wants. Steve is racked with guilt over his failure to provide financial stability for his children, and Evan begins spying on Faith

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the third-round ties, including Celtic v Falkirk at Celtic Park, Stenhousemuir v Kilmarnock at Ochilview Park and Dumbarton v Aberdeen at C&G Systems Stadium, as the sides contested places in the last 16

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City and Chelsea v West Bromwich Albion. The Foxes and the Blues were looking for three points to boost their chances of finishing in the top four, but Leicester faced the toughest possible test against the champions elect. Plus, Arsenal v Liverpool and Leeds United v Sheffield United

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker, Alan Shearer and Micah Richards count down the top 10 Premier League hard men, featuring the likes of Roy Keane, Vinnie Jones, Patrick Vieira and Duncan Ferguson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City and Chelsea v West Bromwich Albion. The Foxes and the Blues were looking for three points to boost their chances of finishing in the top four, but Leicester faced the toughest possible test against the champions elect. Plus, Arsenal v Liverpool and Leeds United v Sheffield United

programme.title: Fern Britton's Holy Land Journey
programme.desc: The presenter travels to Jerusalem on a life-changing journey of faith, in search of the real story of Jesus' final days, from his triumphal arrival, to his brutal crucifixion

programme.title: Easter Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Most Rev Justin Welby shares his Easter message of hope and resurrection, in a joyful celebration, live from the stunning setting of Canterbury Cathedral. Following Covid-safe guidelines, members of Canterbury Cathedral Choir, directed by David Newsholme, sing well-loved hymns and anthems including Jesus Christ is Risen Today, This Joyful Eastertide and Thine be the Glory. The Mass setting is Mozart's Missa Brevis in B Flat. With Gospel music from Junior Garr and members of the Spirituals Choir and readings and prayers from the Rt Rev Dr Rose Hudson-Wilkin, Bishop of Dover, and members of the Community of St Anselm

programme.title: Urbi et Orbi
programme.desc: Pope Francis gives his Easter Message and Blessing on the ninth Easter of his pontificate

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Rosa Bevan has come up with a way of using weeds in cooking and here she teaches viewers how to be wily with them

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: For Easter Sunday, the Moderator of the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland, Rt Rev Martin Fair, is joined by the Archbishop of St Andrews and Edinburgh, Most Rev Leo Cushley. Together they consider the evidence for the Resurrection of Jesus. Music is by Celtic Worship

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: Part two of two. Alexander and Arit Anderson visit gardens expressing hope, joy and renewal. At the Bishop's Palace in Wells, Somerset, Alexander discovers why this place has been sacred for centuries, and helps to create a colourful and uplifting work of art. Arit learns how the ancient trees at Scone Palace in Perthshire will be given a new lease of life, and Alexander heads to his home county of Northumberland to tour a garden using humour to plant seeds of change in the community

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Katherine Jenkins celebrates Easter Sunday from Llandaff Cathedral in Cardiff. She finds out about the meaning behind the Paschal Candle, while James Lusted meets a vicar who has turned his life around. Featuring special performances from Bryn Terfel, Llandaff Cathedral Choir, and Vocal Creation

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a pair of teachers with a £600,000 budget who want to upsize and find the ideal country home for them and their four young children. Away from property, Jonnie turns the heat up on his Lancastrian tour when he visits one of the last working forges in the county

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore finds a pair of seats, an Edwardian armchair and an old typewriter at Witley Recycling Centre in Surrey

programme.title: The Boat Race
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the men's and women's University Boat Races between Oxford and Cambridge, relocated this year to the River Great Ouse in Cambridgeshire. The race was cancelled last year and it was Cambridge who prevailed in both races in 2019 at the traditional venue of the River Thames, with two-time Olympic champion James Cracknell among the members of their men's team, becoming the oldest competitor in the event's history at the age of 46. With analysis from Cracknell, Matthew Pinsent and Katherine Grainger, and commentary by Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison presents the show from the Golden Triangle in Gloucestershire, an area famous for wild daffodils. She talks to local people who remember picking the wild flowers as children and visits some of the finest medieval wall paintings in England. Charlotte Smith investigates the challenges facing rural churches and the communities they serve, and a mother and son reveal how they have fulfilled their dreams of farming in the Cumbrian hills

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Culzean Castle in South West Scotland, and uncovers the story of a former slave who established himself at the heart of the local community. Treasures brought in to be valued include a 1914 FA cup medal, a valuable Art Nouveau clock, and a huge statue found in a compost heap

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness confront the male mid-life crisis by buying the perfect sports cars for a middle-aged gentleman. Back at the track, Chris tests out the raucous new Toyota GR Yaris hot hatch

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: On the strength of evidence from a new witness, Jo brings Terry Boyle back in for questioning about Gail Vella's murder. Kate is torn between loyalty to Jo and wanting to assist AC-12's investigation - and tensions only increase when she encounters a face from the past with links to organised crime

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the latest third-round fixtures, including Rangers v Cove Rangers at Ibrox. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's contests

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Newcastle United v Tottenham Hotspur and Manchester United v Brighton & Hove Albion. Spurs and the Red Devils were looking to boost their chances of qualifying for the Champions League with victories against sides battling against relegation. Plus, Southampton v Burnley, Aston Villa v Fulham and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the latest Super League fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City and Chelsea v Birmingham City

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A married couple from Merthyr Tydfil answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty and Charlie Stayt present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts is in Heslington, a village near York, where he revisits a derelict building where the new owner had some grand plans. Dion Dublin visits Wolverhampton to see a charming bungalow, and Martel Maxwell is in Cardiff to see a first-floor flat

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding recounts the brutal attack of a 96-year-old D-Day war veteran on his doorstep. Police in Taunton, Somerset, quickly arrested his attacker after tracking his spending habits

programme.title: Paramedics on Scene
programme.desc: Special Operations Paramedics Dan and Leigh are called to assist bomb disposal experts after a hazardous chemical is found at a building site. Lee and Nadine race to the scene of a three-vehicle smash on icy roads in Melrose, while paramedics in Oban make two trips to visit a regular patient whose breathing is deteriorating

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Danny Sebastian go antique hunting with two teams at the Royal Cheshire Showground. Presenter Eric Knowles observes proceedings and also discovers the story of two extraordinary Victorian women who brought ancient Egypt to Macclesfield

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The amateur sleuth struggles to protect those closest to him when an old foe is unexpectedly released from prison. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin is in Pembrokeshire with a couple who want not only to buy their first house together, but also need accommodation for holidaymakers and a horse

programme.title: Heavenly Gardens with Alexander Armstrong
programme.desc: The first of two programmes in which the presenter and garden designer Arit Anderson visit gardens of reflection and contemplation. At the remote Pluscarden Abbey in north-east Scotland, Alexander helps harvest luscious apples and accompanies the monks singing midday psalms. Meanwhile, Arit walks in the footsteps of royalty in the glorious grounds of Sudeley Castle. Plus, Alexander returns to his student haunts in Cambridge to discover a place designed to inspire joy, even on the bleakest days, in the university's Botanic Garden

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: A beloved stoneware elephant's fall from a mantlepiece left its sentimental owner shattered into tiny pieces along with the ornament, so ceramics expert Kirsten Ramsay sets about performing a delicate restoration to revive both Michel's precious item and her memories. Plus, Will Kirk and Suzie Fletcher join forces to resurrect a dilapidated child's rocking horse and Brenton West undertakes the repair of an unusual trophy shaped like a boxing ring

programme.title: The Sound of Music (1965)
programme.desc: A cheerful novice nun is employed as governess to the seven children of an austere widower. As she brings light, laughter and a love of all things harmonious to a family previously beset by grief and strict discipline she also falls in love with her employer. But before long, the looming threat of the Second World War jeopardises their happy household. Oscar-winning Rodgers and Hammerstein musical, starring Julie Andrews, Christopher Plummer and Eleanor Parker

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon heads to the gym with the estate agent, where Ben, keen to scupper her plans, makes his own offer on the business. Lily asks Jean if she and the other kids can have an afternoon tea party - but when their gran goes off to buy supplies, the scheming youngster packs a suitcase. Callum's wedding plans are causing problems and embarrassment

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The last challenge of the semi-finals sees the six remaining amateurs cook-off to win a coveted place in the MasterChef Finals. They are tasked to each cook an outstanding dish to impress revered restaurant critics Grace Dent, Jay Rayner and Jimi Famurewa. Serving food to these masters of the dining room is a daunting task for the most adept of chefs, and these talented home-cooks now have to put their skills and palates on the line. Any mistake could cost them the competition, so the pressure is firmly on. It remains to be seen who John Torode and Gregg Wallace will take through to the prestigious finals week

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents a special edition looking back over the best moments from the series. The celebrity panellists joining team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell include Jo Brand, Daisy May Cooper, Stacey Dooley, Ronan Keating, Prue Leith, Scarlett Moffatt, Diane Morgan and Bob Mortimer

programme.title: The Blame Game
programme.desc: Micky Bartlett joins regulars Neil Delamere and Colin Murphy as they raise a socially distanced laugh or two in response to current events. Hosted by Tim McGarry

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Octavia Spencer and Melissa McCarthy, who discuss working together in superhero comedy Thunder Force, while David Schwimmer and Nick Mohammed - co-stars of spy series Intelligence - also appear. Comedian Frank Skinner and singer and star of RuPaul's Drag Race Michelle Visage are also on Graham's guest roster. Plus, Steps perform Heartbreak in This City

programme.title: Spooks: The Greater Good (2015)
programme.desc: A terrorist escapes custody during a prisoner handover when the convoy he is being transported in is attacked, leading to a CIA operative being killed and relations becoming frayed between British and American intelligence agencies. MI5 officer Will Holloway must team up with disgraced intelligence chief Harry Pearce to track him down before an imminent terrorist attack on London. Spy thriller, starring Kit Harington and Peter Firth

programme.desc: A mother and son take on a mother and daughter at the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron help the teams while Anita Manning leads proceedings

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Anu and Ash live in Coventry with their cat Quinn, where their garden is currently a new build blank canvas divided into two cramped tiers. The couple want to turn it into a pet-friendly delight, inspired by the gardens and countryside they fell in love with on holidays in Tuscany, Italy. They want lots of interest for Quinn and a large inviting patio where they can entertain their friends and family. Arit Anderson and Charlie Dimmock have a £3,500 budget to make the couple's dreams come true

programme.desc: Darryl Grimason discovers a remarkable rock pool in Strangford Lough, the famous sea lough 6km from Belfast, whose unique terrain of reefs, mudflats, saltwater rapids and wildlife has led to its designation as a Special Area of Conservation

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the latest Super League fixtures, including Tottenham Hotspur v Manchester City and Chelsea v Birmingham City

programme.title: This Is My House
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley hosts the property-based guessing game in which four people claim to own the same house, while a celebrity panel try work out which one is telling the truth. This week, regulars Bill Bailey, Emily Atack, Judi Love, Jamali Maddix are joined by Rob Rinder to find the real Michael. From the first sight of the four players the panel are instantly thrown by a familiar face, while the path to the truth is obscured by another Michael's inability to remember anything about his own life

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: A married couple from Merthyr Tydfil answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Hosted by Danny Dyer

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint Lords and Commons committee session examining the UK government's review of the Human Rights Act 1998, with evidence from the former president of the UK supreme court Baroness Hale, from Wednesday 3 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to American psychologist professor Laurie Santos, whose work on the science of happiness has won her an audience of millions far beyond the lecture theatre. With Covid putting enormous pressure on mental health, can we really learn how to be happy?

programme.title: Stormont: From Parliament to Power Sharing
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's Faye Kidd presents a brief history of the Northern Ireland legislature from Parliament to power-sharing

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate Coverage of the International Women's Day debate in the House of Commons, from Thursday 11 March.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: SNP MP Douglas Chapman introduces his Bill to the House of Commons under the ten minute rule, covering the automated purchase and resale of gaming hardware to tackle the problem of online 'scalping' relating to games consoles, from Wednesday 24 March.

programme.title: The Treaty of Versailles
programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Conservative party historian Lord Lexden charts the career of Andrew Bonar Law from his birth in Canada to Downing Street, via Pembroke Lodge in Kensington

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Home Affairs Committee session on the work of the UK government's Home Office, with evidence from the home secretary Priti Patel and officials, from Wednesday 24 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the Booker prize-winning author Douglas Stuart. His novel, Shuggie Bain, centres on a boy growing up amid poverty, addiction and intolerance in Glasgow. There are deep parallels with his own life. How does he extract so much love from hardship? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 18 February.

programme.title: Northern Ireland Assembly
programme.desc: Statement in the Assembly on updates to the Covid-19 response and social restrictions in Northern Ireland, with first minister Arlene Foster, from Tuesday 16 March

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Treasury select committee session taking evidence on the 2021 Budget from the Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rishi Sunak

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, observes that the pandemic has forced states to confront how people value health, wealth and opportunity. During the first few months of the crisis, most states chose to value human life more than the economic well-being of the nation-state. But if that seems to be changing how do we assess value in this sense? Dr Carney elucidates surprising differences in the financial value put on a human life in different nations - and goes on to argue that this reductionist approach fails to take into account deeper thinking about the worth of human existence. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: The idea of a social contract between the individual and the state is a staple of political philosophy. But what happens when that contract is threatened by forces beyond the control of any government, like a climate crisis or, right now, a global pandemic? Stephen Sackur speaks to Minouche Shafik, director of the London School of Economics and former top official at the World Bank. Is humanity capable of collective action to meet global challenges?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of a session of the DCMS Committee on the economics of music streaming, with witnesses from Amazon, Apple and Spotify, as well as DCMS minister Caroline Dinenage, from Tuesday 23 February. First shown on BBC Parliament on 27 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: Recorded coverage

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: MPs on the Health and Social Care Committee hear from NHS England Chief Executive Sir Simon Stevens and NHS England Chief Operating Officer Amanda Pritchard, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his final lecture, Mark Carney turns his attention to climate change, arguing that the roots of our environmental emergency lie in a deeper crisis of values

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of the work of committees set up to investigate aspects of public policy and society

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk: Stephen King Stephen Sackur interviews prolific writer Stephen King. He's written more than 60 books and sold close to 400 million copies. Films like the Shining, Carrie and Stand by Me are all based on his stories. He's been writing horror for half a century, but how has our appetite for fear evolved?

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Health and Social Care select committee session on the Health and Social Care White Paper with evidence from the Health Secretary, Matt Hancock, and Jason Yiannikkou, Director of NHS Legislation Programme at Department of Health and Social Care

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: Prime Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Coverage of questions in the House of Commons to Prime Minister Boris Johnson

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint National Security Strategy Committee session on the UK's national security machinery, with evidence from former prime minister David Cameron and former national security advisor Peter Rickets, from Monday 1 March.

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Former Governor of the Bank of England Mark Carney charts how financial value has come to be held in higher esteem than human value, beginning by assessing what value actually means. How looks at whether the way it is assessed both shapes human values and constrains choices, and looks at how the markets affect society. Introduced by Anita Anand

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Despite losing the presidency and both Houses of Congress, Donald Trump still seems to have a chokehold on the Republican party. So what should Republican anti-Trumpers do now? Do they fight from within, or create a new Conservative Party? Stephen Sackur interviews Evan McMullin, one of their key organisers and strategists

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Former prime minister Tony Blair speaking at the Institute for Government on the last twelve months of the Covid-19 pandemic, its lasting impact and the challenges posed for politicians, from Friday 26 February

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: David Cornock tells the story of how Toynbee Hall and London's East End shaped the politics of Labour prime minister Clement Attlee

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Science and Technology select committee session on UK science, research and technology capability and influence in global disease outbreaks with evidence from the Chief Medical Officer for England, Dr Chris Whitty, and the government's Chief Scientific Adviser, Sir Patrick Vallance

programme.title: Scottish First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage of questions to First Minister Nicola Sturgeon in the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Science and Technology Committee session on establishing a new UK research funding agency, with evidence from the former adviser to the prime minister Dominic Cummings, and the business secretary Kwasi Kwarteng, from Wednesday 17 March.

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Donald Trump's presidency is history. So not surprisingly historians are trying to find some perspective on a president who stress-tested American democracy like no other. Stephen Sackur speaks to Timothy Snyder, one of America's leading historians on totalitarianism and the Holocaust. He believes Trump and his movement brought America face to face with early-stage fascism, but is that pushing historical parallels too far? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 22 February.

programme.title: House of Lords
programme.desc: The debate in the House of Lords on the 2021 Budget, from earlier today.

programme.title: Parliamentary Firsts
programme.desc: Selina Seth presents a short guide to the history of diversity in the House of Commons

programme.title: Briefings
programme.desc: Three former chancellors, Norman Lamont, Alistair Darling and George Osborne, in conversation on 'tax in the real world' at the Institute for Government, from Tuesday 9 March

programme.title: Welsh First Minister's Questions
programme.desc: Recorded coverage from the Welsh Assembly as First Minister Mark Drakeford tackles queries on issues affecting life in Wales

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: In his second Reith Lecture, Mark Carney, the former Governor of the Bank of England, takes his audience back to the high drama of the financial crisis of 2008, which ended a period when bankers saw themselves as unassailable Masters of the Universe. More than a decade on, how much have the bankers changed their ways? How far has the financial sector changed? Dr Carney says that society must remain vigilant and resist the 'three lies of finance'. If not, it will live with a system that is ill-prepared for the next crisis. Presented by Anita Anand

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Joint Lords and Commons committee session examining the UK government's review of the Human Rights Act 1998, with evidence from the former president of the UK supreme court Baroness Hale, from Wednesday 3 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Stephen Sackur speaks to American psychologist professor Laurie Santos, whose work on the science of happiness has won her an audience of millions far beyond the lecture theatre. With Covid putting enormous pressure on mental health, can we really learn how to be happy?

programme.title: Stormont: From Parliament to Power Sharing
programme.desc: BBC Parliament's Faye Kidd presents a brief history of the Northern Ireland legislature from Parliament to power-sharing

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by Alicia McCarthy

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Debate Coverage of the International Women's Day debate in the House of Commons, from Thursday 11 March.

programme.title: Ten Minute Rule Bill
programme.desc: SNP MP Douglas Chapman introduces his Bill to the House of Commons under the ten minute rule, covering the automated purchase and resale of gaming hardware to tackle the problem of online 'scalping' relating to games consoles, from Wednesday 24 March.

programme.title: The Treaty of Versailles
programme.desc: Professor Margaret MacMillan delivers a Gresham College lecture looking back at the Treaty of Versailles a hundred years later, from 4 June 2019

programme.title: Prime Properties
programme.desc: Conservative party historian Lord Lexden charts the career of Andrew Bonar Law from his birth in Canada to Downing Street, via Pembroke Lodge in Kensington

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Commons Home Affairs Committee session on the work of the UK government's Home Office, with evidence from the home secretary Priti Patel and officials, from Wednesday 24 February

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: HARDtalk's Stephen Sackur speaks to the Booker prize-winning author Douglas Stuart. His novel, Shuggie Bain, centres on a boy growing up amid poverty, addiction and intolerance in Glasgow. There are deep parallels with his own life. How does he extract so much love from hardship? First shown on the BBC News Channel on 18 February.

programme.desc: As part of BBC Sport's LGBT+ History Month, Clare Balding looks at what it means to be a gay woman in sport. programme.title: I Can't Say My Name: Stammering in the Spotlight
programme.desc: BBC producer Felicity Baker has a stammer that she has spent her whole life trying to hide. She struggles to say her own name. Now she is stepping out in front of the camera for the first time to talk about what it is like to live with the condition - and she soon finds she is not alone. She meets a rugby player who blames his stammer for his aggressive behaviour on the pitch, a rapper who found music stopped him stammering and the comedy star Michael Palin, whose father stammered but never ever spoke about it

programme.title: Our World
programme.desc: Human rights activist Azimjan Askarov was imprisoned in Kyrgyzstan in 2010 for a crime he says he did not commit. Ever since then, his wife Hadicha has campaigned tirelessly for his release. Now she sees one last chance for justice, in an appeal hearing at the country's supreme court. But as Hadicha prepares for the verdict, coronavirus is spreading across Kyrgyzstan, stopping Hadicha's prison visits and putting 69-year-old Azimjan at risk. Our World follows an extraordinary story of love, courage - and never giving up.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: An undercover investigation inside a lab analysing thousands of Covid-19 tests a day. Secretly filmed footage reveals a failing service with shoddy practices where staff complain they are under pressure to meet targets despite the lab often running well below capacity. The programme discovers there have been three outbreaks of coronavirus among staff and that social distancing is poorly maintained. Test samples sometimes arrive poorly packaged and labelled, with equipment frequently malfunctioning, leading to contamination of results. The programme also discovers that tests, including some intended to find new variants of coronavirus, have been wrongly discarded or lost

programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: In for a Penny
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show from locations around the UK, setting members of the public unusual challenges for a chance to win anything from the price of their petrol, to £1000 in the big finale game. This week, Stephen is in Bristol

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The village grieves with the loss of life, Mack is guilt-ridden, and Rodney makes a confession

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyrone tells Alina that for the sake of his family, she needs to move away. Corey puts pressure on Asha to go to bed with him, and after Dev kicks him out, Alahan informs his daughter that Seb is now dating Nina

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: Wealthy betting shop tycoon Alun Wilmott is shot and killed during a home invasion, leaving his wife and daughter traumatised - with large amounts of cash missing from his personal safe, it is assumed he is the unlucky victim of a burglary. Vera suspects an inside job in the shape of Alun's head of private security, Ciaran Duggan, whose history of violent crime calls his reformed character into question. Can the team find the evidence to pin the crime on their prime suspect - or are they looking in the wrong place? Brenda Blethyn stars, with guest appearances from Karl Collins, Patrick Robinson and Katy Carmichael

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Johnny Briggs: Coronation Street Legend
programme.desc: A tribute to the actor who spent years acting in projects such as The Lavender Hill Mob, 633 Squadron and Crossroads, before becoming a Weatherfield regular. Johnny Briggs MBE spent 30 years playing Cockney businessman and Lothario Mike Baldwin, eventually leaving the soap in 2006, after appearing in more than 1,600 episodes. Street stars past and present recall working with a cast legend, and famous fans of the show take a fond look back at some of Mike's biggest storylines, including his affair with Deirdre Barlow and bitter rivalry with her husband Ken, a love triangle that had the whole nation gripped

programme.title: Beat the Chasers
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as famous faces take on quiz geniuses Mark Labbett, Anne Hegerty, Shaun Wallace, Jenny Ryan and Darragh Ennis, hoping to win big money for their chosen charities

Jordan, Katherine Ryan, Will Ferrell, Demi Lovato, Sacha Baron Cohen, Ashley Banjo and Patrick Dempsey

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Gran sits in front of the television for so long that she turns into a cocoon, and with a little help from Dave - who wants his favourite seat back - she hatches into a moth. When Moth-Gran sets her sights on the moon, it's up to the Spuds to find a way to entice her back.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James serves up an Easter feast for ex-football manager Harry Redknapp, including beef, treacle tart and a delicious simnel cake. There are amazing creations from award-winning chocolatier Mark Tilling and recipes to try at home from chefs Michael Caines and Sally Abe

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his favourite dishes for anniversaries, date nights and romantic get-togethers. He starts with broad beans with parmesan and pan-fried sole, and ends with an exotic fruit pannacotta. Plus, chef and good friend Paul Ainsworth cooks a special dish for Raymond - lamb hotpot for two

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Fellow chef Paul Rankin gives James a personal tour of Northern Ireland's capital city - his home town of Belfast - before the duo enjoy a spot of golf. James pitches up his mobile kitchen right on the green to whip up a tasty dish with bacon, scallops and apples with onion rings, and later cooks a Northern Irish classic - beef with champ, charred onions and parsley oil. Next, James is in Craigavon to visit Danni Barry, the first woman named the best chef in Ireland, who rustles up a dessert using local ingredients. Finally, the presenter visits Strangford Lough to fish for langoustines

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill presents more digital disasters. In this episode, the narrator casts his eye upon an unexpected eyeful, a man holding on for survival and a wandering trifle

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard welcomes funny man Richard Herring, actor Joe McFadden and reality star Olivia Attwood. They answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking celebrity contestants Jeff Stelling, Charlotte Hawkins and Andy Whyment to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: Comedian Mo Gilligan is in the guest announcer booth, while athlete Mo Farah is Ant and Dec's latest victim in I'm a Celebrity Get Out of Me Ear. Plus, Stephen Mulhern is on hand for the climax of Ant v Dec: Go for Gold, comedy serial Double Trouble draws to an epic conclusion and the programme goes out with a bang thanks to a spectacular End of the Show Show

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian returns with the programme in which some of the nation's all-time favourite game shows are rebooted, beginning with a star-studded version of Play Your Cards Right. Couples including Harry and Sandra Redknapp, and Martin and Shirlie Kemp take part to be in with a chance of winning the jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: It's Clarkson on TV
programme.desc: Some television is better than ever, while some of it is atrocious - and Jeremy Clarkson is going to say exactly what he makes of it. In this programme, he unpicks popular TV events and unearths wonderful hidden gems, with an eye for detail and a rogue sense of humour

programme.desc: A wannabe police officer persuades his girlfriend's tough detective brother to take him along on duty. The cop hopes to scare his unwanted partner away from his sister by exposing him to danger - but needs some help when the patrol leads to a run-in with the city's most wanted criminal. Comedy, starring Ice Cube, Kevin Hart and Laurence Fishburne

There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave and his friend Zombie Katie are working on a school project together about endangered animals, so George takes them on an expedition to the local park to see what they can find. There they discover the much-fabled Beast of Grimsby - and Dave must decide between a gold star at school and the future of the beast.

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia chooses six of her favourite and most accessible family treks from across the UK, in search of the perfect scenic walk that anyone can manage. She begins by heading to one of the most famous landmarks on the Jurassic Coast - Old Harry Rocks in Dorset, which boasts spectacular views towards the Isle of Wight and of the offshore chalk stacks, good opportunities for bird and butterfly watching and a rich variety of wild flowers in spring and summer. There are also miles of golden sandy beaches and a dramatic vista of the bay

programme.desc: Penniless Charlie Bucket is one of five lucky children who find a golden ticket in a chocolate bar, winning a once-in-a-lifetime tour of eccentric recluse Willy Wonka's magical sweet company. Unfortunately, the other winners each face a grim fate when they start to misbehave. programme.desc: Wealthy betting shop tycoon Alun Wilmott is shot and killed during a home invasion, leaving his wife and daughter traumatised - with large amounts of cash missing from his personal safe, it is assumed he is the unlucky victim of a burglary. Vera suspects an inside job in the shape of Alun's head of private security, Ciaran Duggan, whose history of violent crime calls his reformed character into question. Can the team find the evidence to pin the crime on their prime suspect - or are they looking in the wrong place? Brenda Blethyn stars, with guest appearances from Karl Collins, Patrick Robinson and Katy Carmichael

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Johnny Briggs: Coronation Street Legend
programme.desc: A tribute to the actor who spent years acting in projects such as The Lavender Hill Mob, 633 Squadron and Crossroads, before becoming a Weatherfield regular. Johnny Briggs MBE spent 30 years playing Cockney businessman and Lothario Mike Baldwin, eventually leaving the soap in 2006, after appearing in more than 1,600 episodes. Street stars past and present recall working with a cast legend, and famous fans of the show take a fond look back at some of Mike's biggest storylines, including his affair with Deirdre Barlow and bitter rivalry with her husband Ken, a love triangle that had the whole nation gripped

programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Gran sits in front of the television for so long that she turns into a cocoon, and with a little help from Dave - who wants his favourite seat back - she hatches into a moth. When Moth-Gran sets her sights on the moon, it's up to the Spuds to find a way to entice her back.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James serves up an Easter feast for ex-football manager Harry Redknapp, including beef, treacle tart and a delicious simnel cake. There are amazing creations from award-winning chocolatier Mark Tilling and recipes to try at home from chefs Michael Caines and Sally Abe

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef shares his favourite dishes for anniversaries, date nights and romantic get-togethers. He starts with broad beans with parmesan and pan-fried sole, and ends with an exotic fruit pannacotta. Plus, chef and good friend Paul Ainsworth cooks a special dish for Raymond - lamb hotpot for two

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Fellow chef Paul Rankin gives James a personal tour of Northern Ireland's capital city - his home town of Belfast - before the duo enjoy a spot of golf. James pitches up his mobile kitchen right on the green to whip up a tasty dish with bacon, scallops and apples with onion rings, and later cooks a Northern Irish classic - beef with champ, charred onions and parsley oil. Next, James is in Craigavon to visit Danni Barry, the first woman named the best chef in Ireland, who rustles up a dessert using local ingredients. Finally, the presenter visits Strangford Lough to fish for langoustines

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is reunited with old friend Ariadne Oliver, who has been commissioned to stage a game of murder hunt at a summer fete in the grounds of Nasse House, the recently purchased home of wealthy financier Sir George Stubbs. However, the novelist's instincts tell her she's getting involved in something much darker - and she's proved right when the girl volunteering to play the part of the victim really does turn out to be dead. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Zoe Wanamaker, Sean Pertwee, Martin Jarvis, Sam Kelly, Sinead Cusack, Rosalind Ayres and Tom Ellis

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill presents more digital disasters. In this episode, the narrator casts his eye upon an unexpected eyeful, a man holding on for survival and a wandering trifle

One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice
