programme.title: Brojects in the House
programme.desc: Brothers Andrew and Kevin turn traditional home and cottage renovation projects on their head

programme.title: Brojects in the House
programme.desc: Brothers Andrew and Kevin turn traditional home and cottage renovation projects on their head

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike cannot resist making an offer on retro boat and a BMW micro car at a garage in California, and later, he and Frank comb through a huge stockpile at a radiator shop

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: The chef visits the town of Madurai in Tamil Nadu, where he learns the art of temple cooking and samples south Indian dish sambar. He then takes a road trip across the Western Ghats to the state of Kerala, where he prepares regional dessert payasam and a traditional pork curry

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May visit Africa on a mission to find the definitive source of the Nile, the world's longest river, which spans more than 4,000 miles. The trio believe they can do a better job than explorers who claim to have discovered the waterway's starting point by travelling further and faster than previous efforts - but the only tools at their disposal are determination, ingenuity and three ageing estate cars

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Rhod Gilbert and Greg Davies negotiate a variety of unusual hazards as they travel through Nepal, a country with one of the world's highest rates of road traffic accidents. Frequent floods and landslides mean car journeys are often fraught with danger, and matters are not helped by the varied states of Nepalese roads, which range from urban highways to crumbling mountain passes

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Festive edition from 2008 in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on one of the most spectacular road trips they have ever attempted. Arriving in Vietnam, they are challenged to travel the entire length of the country in just eight days. First they must buy suitable transport for the journey, and despite having several million Dong in their pockets, this proves more difficult than they expected, especially in one of the most expensive car markets in the world

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The team sets off on a high-altitude trek from Kathmandu, capital of Nepal, to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, a secretive kingdom perched high on the Tibetan plateau, on the far side of the Himalayas. On a five-day expedition through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet, Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris battle huge river crossings, vast mudslides and dizzying mountain passes on a trip that would test even the hardiest new 4x4s to the limit. But Paddy, Freddie and Chris aren't crossing the Himalayas in new 4x4s. They're crossing the Himalayas in a small old Peugeot, a very compact old Renault, and Nepal's first (and only) home-built car

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania 2 (2015)
programme.desc: Dracula is worried that his half-human grandson is not showing any vampiric tendencies, so enlists the help of his fellow monsters in showing the youngster how it is done. The count is shocked to receive an unwelcome visit from his own father, who does not approve of his son's tolerance of humans. Animated comedy, with the voices of Adam Sandler and Andy Samberg

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy take the children out trick or treating for Halloween, but the night soon turns disastrous for Lee when he loses his mobile on the porch of a spooky Victorian house. After returning to the property to retrieve his phone, Lee discovers that the building is full of secrets that would be far better left undisturbed

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee and Lucy pop out on a last-minute Christmas Eve mission, leaving their youngsters in the apparently capable hands of Geoffrey - cutting more of a strict and stuffy Victorian gent than usual - and wife Wendy. Alas, things don't quite go to plan for the outward-bound duo, as a theft and unexpected trip into deep countryside await. Not only that, but there are encounters with an aggressive bull in a field and a terrifying truck driver to endure. Meanwhile, back at home, Lee's lovably feckless father Frank turns up to offer gifts - in his own inimitably festive way. Lee Mack, Sally Bretton and Bobby Ball star

programme.title: S.W.A.T. (2003)
programme.desc: A cop's career is seemingly over when he accidentally shoots a hostage while thwarting a bank robbery. He is given the chance to redeem himself by joining an elite unit headed by a veteran sergeant. Their mission is to escort an international crime lord in custody as he is taken to prison. However, they hit a snag when their prisoner offers a multimillion-dollar reward to anyone who breaks him out of jail, placing them under attack from every criminal in the city. Action thriller, starring Samuel L Jackson and Colin Farrell

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Chef Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and comedians Rhod Gilbert, Rufus Hound and Miranda Hart are the guests on the comedy panel show. Team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack guide the contestants as they try to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Presented by Rob Brydon

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner presents the light-hearted panel show, which sees comedy actress Miranda Hart, TV and radio host Reggie Yates and Countryfile presenter John Craven competing to have their pet hates banished to the infamous room for ever. Their gripes include stuffed marrows, spitting, the hip-hop handshake and Miranda's breasts

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Last week saw the return of the comedy show in which three comedians take the stage of the Hammersmith Apollo in west London. The premise is simple. One comic acts as host, warming up the audience before introducing short sets by two other stand-ups. Future editions promise the likes of Ed Byrne, Rob Beckett, Joel Dommett, Henning Wehn, Lucy Porter, but tonight Nish Kumar plays host, before handing over to David O'Doherty and Luisa Omielan

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Mel Giedroyc takes over the reins for the extended edition of the topical quiz, with comedian Hal Cruttenden and writer Sathnam Sanghera joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: Police crack down on the gangs that use local businesses as a front for selling drugs, executing raids on these small-time dealers in the hope of crippling the larger players

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: In the Philippines, President Rodrigo Duterte declares open season on anyone caught dealing or using drugs. Soon after, the DEA heads out on a massive multi-target drugs raid

programme.title: Secrets of the Supercars
programme.desc: A look at the Pagani Huayra Roadster and its production facility in Italy. Plus, the Morgan 44 Drophead Couple and the Fiat S76 feature

programme.title: World's Most Dangerous Roads
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Hugh Dennis undertake a perilous journey through the Peruvian Andes. Their route takes in 2,000ft drops, one-lane dirt roads, remote mountain trails and muddy, humid jungle terrain, as they search for the lost city of Constitucion, once destined to be the capital of Peru

programme.title: Red Bull Soapbox Race: USA's Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Highlights of some of the best, wackiest and craziest soapboxes and the biggest and most spectacular crashes from over the years and many competitions in the USA

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Special in which Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May take three used, V8-engined sports cars on a gruelling road trip covering 1,600 miles through Patagonia to the world's southernmost city. Having encountered stunning landscapes, mechanical mishaps and dreadful roads on their journey through Argentina, the trio's adventure is brought to a dramatic climax by a terrifying turn of events

programme.title: Secrets of the Supercars
programme.desc: A look at off road vehicles, including the Land Rover Defender, and Titch Cormack is in Cornwall uncovering the WW2 era Willys Jeep

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fly to Enniskillen, Co Fermanagh, to fish for pike, which eluded them in the previous series. They take to the waters of Lough Erne in their search for this mighty fish, guided by the very experienced Pascal. They discuss their respective bad habits and Bob gives Paul a thorough health update, before they retire to a local hostelry to sample traditional refreshments. Paul demonstrates how to perform life-saving CPR and Bob gives him a glimpse into his future by telling his fortune in an ancient Irish manner

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Countdown presenter (and Apprentice sidekick) Nick Hewer squares off against his show's former maths whizz Carol Vorderman and comedian Rhod Gilbert, making the case with host Frank Skinner for their gripes and pet hates to be banished for ever. The trio's dislikes include train managers, camping, Facebook, golf, holidays and advertising slogans

programme.title: Room 101
programme.desc: Frank Skinner presents the light-hearted panel show, which sees comedy actress Miranda Hart, TV and radio host Reggie Yates and Countryfile presenter John Craven competing to have their pet hates banished to the infamous room for ever. Their gripes include stuffed marrows, spitting, the hip-hop handshake and Miranda's breasts

programme.title: Hypothetical
programme.desc: Josh Widdicombe and James Acaster pose ridiculous Hypothetical scenarios to Sue Perkins, Sara Pascoe, Dane Baptiste and Sindhu Vee

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Last week saw the return of the comedy show in which three comedians take the stage of the Hammersmith Apollo in west London. The premise is simple. One comic acts as host, warming up the audience before introducing short sets by two other stand-ups. Future editions promise the likes of Ed Byrne, Rob Beckett, Joel Dommett, Henning Wehn, Lucy Porter, but tonight Nish Kumar plays host, before handing over to David O'Doherty and Luisa Omielan

programme.title: Dave Gorman: Terms and Conditions Apply
programme.desc: Using his trademark, powerpoint-comedy and mischievous games, Dave invites David O'Doherty, Rose Matafeo and Ed Gamble to help him make sense of the world

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Devious Greg Davies tries to spot our five camouflaged comics, before the gang attempt to herd some dogs and chickens

programme.title: Alan Davies: As Yet Untitled
programme.desc: Susan Calman, Sophie Willan, Ivo Graham and Sanjeev Bhaskar join Alan to tackle pressing issues such as Scotland, tin foil pants and instant mash

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian takes a job in a hair and beauty salon, quickly believing himself ready to work on real customers as he tries an all-over tan and perfects his own unique version of the bob

programme.title: Drug Wars
programme.desc: Hundreds of cops in Quezon City in the Philippines carry out a massive anti-narcotics operation in one of the city's slums - and this time they are targeting both dealers and users

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the history of personal flight, culminating in Richard Browning and his team of engineers' development of the Gravity Industries Jet Suit

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: An insight into a team of engineers in Perth's attempts to not only break the 763mph land speed record set back in 1997, but also go past the 1,000mph barrier

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: The story of the invertebrates that left the sea to move on to land 400 million years ago. Since then, they have become the most successful group of animals, without which the land's ecosystem would collapse. David Attenborough examines the insects' behaviour, travelling to South Africa and Venezuela to witness the habits and routines of millipedes and centipedes

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: David Attenborough tells the tale of winged insects, witnessing mayflies emerging from their larval cases in central Hungary and purple crow butterflies in the valleys of Taiwan. Slow-motion cameras reveal the incredible flight skills that enable the creatures to find new territories and sources of food. The aeronautical tricks employed by heavier insects such as bees and beetles are also revealed

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: An exploration of how invertebrates' production of silk 300 million years ago became their greatest invention. Stronger than a steel thread of the same diameter, the material is as pliable as elastic and employed by spiders for activities as diverse as courtship, catching prey and tending their young. David Attenborough reveals how some communal spiders create silken palaces rising 15m to 20m into the forest canopy

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: David Attenborough turns his attention to the network of partnerships that invertebrates have developed with one another and with plants. He reveals how the fairy wasp, the world's smallest insect, flies underwater to inject its eggs into those of the water beetle. Footage also shows how the caterpillar of the blue butterfly smuggles its way into an ants' nest by mimicking the smell of their young

programme.title: Life in the Undergrowth
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores how insects compensate for their small size by banding together in vast numbers. He reveals that the operations of many of these societies are so finely tuned they appear to work as a single entity. Footage shows how African termite mounds act as huge ventilation systems of such sophistication that humans are only now beginning to unravel the mechanics of the structure

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The varied landscape of the tropical islands and the species that inhabit them, from flamingos and four-eyed fish to anteaters and cave-dwelling rodents. The programme also delves deeper to explore the islands' destructive history of volcanic eruptions, dangerous reefs and powerful hurricanes

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The coral and shipwrecks found off the shores of the tropical islands, and how the different kinds of reef have given rise to a huge diversity of underwater life in the area. Underwater time-lapse photography also shows how carnivorous corals feed at night and how, over a few nights of the year, they disperse their sperm and eggs

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The effects of the region's powerful hurricanes on the islands' wildlife and the survival stories of some of the species, including how the Puerto Rican parrot almost became extinct. Offshore, lobsters head to deeper waters for refuge, while reefs benefit from the destruction

programme.title: Wild Caribbean
programme.desc: The region's mysterious side comes to light during a journey from the rainforests of Panama to the Barrier Reef of the Americas - the world's second largest coral system. The programme also explores the wildlife on the shores of Central America, including killer whales and thimble jellyfish in the deep waters near Honduras

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, beginning with London to Monte Carlo. His first stop is Paris, where he absorbs the atmosphere, before heading south to the Cote d'Azur and ending his journey at Monaco's gaming tables

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his trusty copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, Michael Portillo explores the former domain of the Habsburg monarchs, beginning in the Hungarian capital Budapest. He then travels to Austria via Bratislava in Slovakia, and in Vienna he immerses himself in pre-First World War decadence. From there he heads to Salzburg, before going on to the Salzkammergut region to visit the emperor's Austrian summer residence at Bad Ischl, where in 1914 Franz Joseph I signed a declaration that changed the course of European history

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Germany, beginning his journey in Berlin - which at the beginning of the 20th Century was a powerhouse of science and technology. Led by his copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide of 1913, he heads west via the Harz Mountains to the industrial Ruhr Valley to learn how imperial Germany was ready for war. He then goes south along the tourist trail of the castle-studded Rhine river and ends in the Rheingau to taste the wines of its vineyards

programme.title: Warbird Workshop
programme.desc: A group of amateur pilots find their light plane has a glorious war record - and launch an ambitious project to rebuild the Piper Cub and restore its military markings

programme.title: Secrets of the Transport Museum
programme.desc: A historic Frazer Nash car must be fixed before a big race. Plus, the first car to go round the track is resurrected but will the museum director be able to drive it?

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave goes to Wales to bag a Sierra Cosworth, a sought-after car in the bidding market. Dad Derek gets his hands on a more sedate 1926 Standard Tourer - a quirky little motor

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The team is under pressure with a record number of cars to auction, but Derek finds room for one more - a replica AC Cobra. A couple are reunited with their Volvo breakdown truck

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A classic car fan with a love of the Jaguar E-Type reluctantly sends his dream car to auction, hoping to buy a motorhome with the money. Two Ford Escorts get Dave's pulse racing

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The captain of HMS Queen Elizabeth deliberately seeks out the worst possible weather so he can test how the F35 jets cope under extreme conditions - and gets his wish when the aircraft carrier ends up in the path of a hurricane. After consulting with his officers, he resolves to brave the elements and test the jets to the brink of destruction. The crew also faces the challenge of refueling the vessel at sea - one of the hardest tasks outside of going into battle

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: The crew of HMS Queen Elizabeth completes the vessel's sea trials, testing an innovative new firefighting system for the first time and marking the 100th anniversary of a plane being successfully landed on a moving ship. The carrier returns to Portsmouth, which will be its home port for the next 50 years, while the pilots of its fighter jets go to the US for additional training

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look onboard the $200million Black Pearl, which is the largest sailing yacht in the world and has been built to sail faster than any ship of its size

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: A look at the Qinghai-Tibet line, the highest railway in the world, providing an insight into the engineering challenges faced at high altitude and in bitterly cold temperatures

programme.title: Wild Japan
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the wildlife of rural Japan's mountains, rainforests, beaches and swamps, beginning with Honshu, Japan's largest island

programme.title: Wild Japan
programme.desc: The tropical Southwest Islands in the south of Japan, which are home to volcanoes and fierce typhoons

programme.title: Wild Japan
programme.desc: The wildlife of rural Japan's mountains, rainforests and beaches, where the climate can shift drastically from icy cold winters to tropical summers

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: High-definition cameras and aerial photography techniques are used to explore the archipelago, which is rich in wildlife and made famous by Charles Darwin's On the Origin of the Species. This programme focuses on the geological history of the islands through the evidence of volcanic eruptions and examines what makes it unique. Tilda Swinton narrates

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: Examining the lesser-known parts of the islands and the species that have successfully evolved to suit the environment. The programme discovers how tortoise shells have changed shape, flowers have turned yellow to attract bees, and finches have become warblers. Plus, how flightless cormorants are still hunting underwater. Narrated by Tilda Swinton

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: An insight into how creatures adapt to the islands' extremes of weather, including iguanas that climb more than 1,000 metres to lay eggs, fish forced to rely on the shade provided by mangroves, and seals that survive on exposed shorelines by taking shelter in lava grottos

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: In central Thailand's forests, fertile plains and even its city streets, nature finds a way of living alongside people, and spirituality can be found in human and animal relationships, both likely and unlikely. This bustling region is known as the nation's rice bowl - but even here, there are magical places to be found. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: The mountains and forests of Northern Thailand, an area that is home to a variety of wildlife, leading to unusual partnerships developing between animals and people. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Following in the footsteps of early 20th-century travellers, Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to explore Switzerland, which was a favourite with Edwardian tourists. He begins in Basel, travels east to visit the industrial regions of Zurich and learns about the engineering feats needed to thread the rail network through the Alps. He takes in the striking beauty of Lake Lucerne, before ending with an ascent by train to Europe's highest station at Jungfraujoch

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo uses his 1913 copy of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide to explore Holland and Belgium, before travelling to the French sector of the Western Front. He ends his journey in the Forest of Compiegne in northern France to hear how the First World War armistice was signed in a railway carriage

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo once again ventures onto the European rail network to retrace journeys featured in George Bradshaw's 1913 publication Continental Guide. He begins in Spain, where he visits the scene of an assassination attempt at a royal wedding in Madrid, before dancing with an unusual partner in Cordoba. He investigates the story behind Bizet's Carmen at a tobacco factory in Seville, learns about Winston Churchill's tense diplomatic mission to Algeciras and finishes with tales of British espionage in Gibraltar

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletcher helps cellmate Godber make it through the night, calling on his unpredictable and limitless imagination to while away the hours. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: The inmates face a day of hard labour digging drains on a lonely stretch of moorland. However, Fletcher soon finds a way of turning the situation to his advantage. Prison comedy, starring Ronnie Barker and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: Porridge
programme.desc: Fletch desperately wants to get out of the tedious task of making fishing nets, and hatches a crafty scheme to get himself assigned to the library. Classic comedy, starring Ronnie Barker

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of guitarists taken from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Includes electric performances by Carlos Santana, Mark Knopfler, Peter Green and the Edge, and acoustic material by John Martyn, John Renbourn, Bert Jansch and Paco Pena

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: A celebration of 1970s-era axe-men, acoustic virtuosos and thumping riff merchants, in a compilation of guitar-heavy performances from the BBC TV archives. Guitar gods including Jimi Hendrix, Pete Townshend, Peter Green and Johnny Winter are joined by, among others, flamenco maestro Manitas De Plata, bottleneck bluesman Ry Cooder and straight-up rockers AC/DC and Thin Lizzy. Plus, Fleetwood Mac, the Jam and Be Bop Deluxe and Nils Lofgren

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Status Quo play Pictures of Matchstick Men on Top of the Pops in 1968, the Who perform Long Live Rock in the Old Grey Whistle Test studio, Dire Straits deliver Tunnel of Love and Lynyrd Skynyrd bring a taste of the American South with Sweet Home Alabama. The programme also features rare performances from George Benson, Leo Kottke, Link Wray and Tom Petty

programme.title: Viking Dead
programme.desc: Experts analyse the content of Viking burial sites to learn more about Norse culture, beginning with the mass grave of a raiding party, which dates back to Roman times

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: Exploring the wildlife and culture of the Southeast Asian nation. The first episode examines the south of the country, a popular tourist destination for Westerners, and examines the role of spirituality in daily life. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: In central Thailand's forests, fertile plains and even its city streets, nature finds a way of living alongside people, and spirituality can be found in human and animal relationships, both likely and unlikely. This bustling region is known as the nation's rice bowl - but even here, there are magical places to be found. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: Thailand: Earth's Tropical Paradise
programme.desc: The mountains and forests of Northern Thailand, an area that is home to a variety of wildlife, leading to unusual partnerships developing between animals and people. Narrated by Sophie Okonedo

programme.title: India's New Worlds
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary charting wildlife conservation projects in India, including efforts to avert the extinction of the Asiatic lion

programme.title: India's New Worlds
programme.desc: Concluding part of the documentary charting some of the many wildlife conservation projects currently being pursued in India

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Rare footage of the elusive animals that live in the unforgiving environment of the world's mountain ranges. A female snow leopard with a cub ends up caught between two rival males, grizzly bears rub themselves up against trees in an attempt to shed their winter fur, and golden eagles hunt over snow-capped peaks

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of plants and animals that live in jungles, one of the hardest environments to thrive in, including strange fungi and creatures that glow in the dark which have never been captured on film before. The programme also includes recordings of caimans and the jaguars that prey on them, freshwater dolphins that swim through flooded forests, undergrowth-dwelling frogs that have to defend themselves against wasps, and the Indri, the world's largest living lemur, which inhabits the forests of Madagascar

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of animals that live in the desert, including a pride of desert lions that risks hunting a giraffe several times their size. The film also features an immense swarm of locusts, sand grouse flying for 120 miles to gather water for their offspring, and an unprecedented recording of a tiny bat defending itself against one of the world's deadliest scorpions

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Wildlife that inhabit the world's grasslands, areas that are struck by some of the most dramatic seasonal changes seen in the world. The programme features footage of Brazil's giant anteaters and Asian saiga antelope, as well as lions engaging in ferocious battles with buffalo in the flooded Okavango, bee-eaters taking advantage of elephants to help them catch insects, and caribou embarking on migrations across the freezing northern tundra, stalked every step of the way by predatory Arctic wolves

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: Footage of wild animals that have made a home in cities, including leopards on the streets of Mumbai, peregrine falcons hunting among New York's skyscrapers, hyenas that live peacefully alongside the people of Harar, macaques in Jaipur, and langurs in Jodhpur, which are regarded as having great religious significance by the citizens. The programme also examines how some animals are struggling to survive in urban environments, and questions how cities can be built to serve their needs as well as those of the human inhabitants

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his 1913 railway guide, Michael Portillo travels through Italy from Turin to Venice.Along the way, he recreates a scene from the Michael Caine film The Italian Job on a Fiat test track and takes a trip in a seaplane to investigate the early 20th-century British love affair with Lake Como. In Verona, he visits the house used to attract Edwardian tourists to the alleged setting of Shakespeare's Romeo and Juliet and finds a microcosm of pre-First World War Europe at the Venice Biennale art exhibition

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through Germany, learning about an Edwardian fitness craze in Dresden before heading to Leipzig on a historic line built by British engineers in 1839. He discovers model-railway making on the grandest of scales in Hamburg and ends his journey in Kiel, where he reveals how British yachtsmen spied on the German navy in the early 20th century

Next, Sue Burston from The Falcon Retreat wants to get her claws into her guests' breakfast blues

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Maidenhead, Simon O'Brien falls in love with an old ship's carpenter's chest, while Henry Cole takes away a miniature Tether car, which he hopes to get started

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Max McMurdo and Simon O'Brien are in Woking, where they find a sidecar to be transformed into a boat and a water cylinder that Simon might make a trendy lamp

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Herefordshire to meet former farmer Julian. Henry finds an old cattle feeder that he wants to turn into a garden bench, while Simon hatches plans to turn a vintage seed drill into a trendy dining table

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Simon O'Brien and Henry Cole visit Witney in Oxfordshire, where Henry comes across a tractor that has not run for quite a few years - but poses no problem for restorer Alan

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jane and Martin want a holiday home in the town of Portimão in the Algarve - but Ben Hillman needs to find properties suitable not only for the couple but their beloved dog Bailey

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Kelly from Essex would love a holiday home in the Black Sea coastal resort of Sunny Beach in Bulgaria. She's looking for an apartment on a complex, with plenty of facilities and a pool. With a budget of £25,000 and Kelly's best friend Angie coming along to help, will any of Scarlette Douglas's five fantastic properties turn out to be the perfect package?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Recently retired Baksho wants to buy a holiday home in the resort of Gran Alacant on Spain's popular Costa Blanca. With a budget of £100,000 and her sister Sukvinder by her side to help, Jasmine Harman sets out to find Baksho the perfect family friendly property

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel Strawbridge turn their attentions to the wash house - a shell of an old stone building on the far side of the moat. They have visions of turning it into a bathroom for their 40-foot boat on the moat, complete with all eco-friendly mod cons. And it's not long before the whole family get to work clearing the overgrown site so the project can begin

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at two shows featuring families who needed to reconfigure their ground-floor layouts. Graham and Nardeth bought their detached Windsor home 10 years ago, but while he loves Windsor and sees it as their dream home, she is tired of the cramped interior. Enter Kirstie, knocking down walls in the hope of proving the house was worth keeping. Plus, Northamptonshire couple Louis and Isobel are desperate to enjoy their retirement - but it will take a £70,000 refurbishment to reconfigure the ground floor. Even then, will they decide to stay or sell up?

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: The police investigate an apparently unprovoked attack that has left a man fighting for his life after being shot in the chest at point blank range by a complete stranger. As Bedfordshire Police's Guns & Gangs unit comes under increasing pressure to catch the shooter before he strikes again, a woman arrives at Luton police station to report being threatened by a man who matches the description

programme.title: First Dates Hotel
programme.desc: Six-foot gym owner and ex-military man Richard is paired with Emilia. Despite his muscular appearance, he is a sensitive soul who loves musicals and Harry Potter, and his Asperger's leaves him unable to read what others are thinking, so mid-date he is forced to ask Emilia how he is doing. Meanwhile, 40-year-old hotelier Andy arrives at Vesuvius for his first ever date with a man, 37-year-old bouncer Joseph, and police officer Angie hooks up with car salesman Tom, who is determined to leave his stereotypical lad image behind

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. Steve, 52, is rushed to St George's after falling over and hitting his head on a concrete step, while 21-year-old Fleur is assessed after she woke up with pain and swelling in her leg. Muffy, 72, is brought to Resus with slurred speech and weakness in her legs, and doctors suspect she may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The armchair critics share their opinions on what they have been watching during the week, as cameras capture their instant reactions prompted by the week's biggest and best shows, be they entertainment juggernauts, hard-hitting documentaries, news reports or gritty dramas

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart plays a prank on Mrs Krabappel, but it backfires badly and results in her being fired, leaving him feeling so guilty that he resolves to do everything he can to get her reinstated - even if it means discrediting her replacement

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: During a trip to a water park with the family, Grampa bumps into a newspaper columnist who seemingly becomes fascinated with his life and writes a series of stories about him, but Homer becomes suspicious of the journalist's motives

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Dracula, the Wolf Man, the Mummy and Frankenstein's monster gatecrash a Halloween party at 742 Evergreen Terrace. Bart and Lisa plot revenge on their teachers in a Hitchcock-inspired short story, but end up turning on each another

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge poses for a charity calendar and becomes Springfield's number-one sex symbol, while Carl gets a promotion at the plant, before going on a power trip and hiring Homer as his new assistant

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa accepts an invitation to join a Wiccan coven - but before she can be initiated, Chief Wiggum arrives and arrests the other members on charges of witchcraft. Meanwhile, Homer befriends local hillbilly Cletus, eager to share his plentiful supply of illegal moonshine liquor

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The week's first visit is to Carly Cornwall's Juniperlea Inn in Midlothian, where the roadside location causes sleepless nights and one guest announces that they would not return

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals arrive at Starshinezzz in County Durham, where laid-back host Dave Taylor hopes his renowned hospitality will make him a winner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests are charmed by Barney Smith's Hobbit-style accommodation at the Hideaway at Baxby Manor in North Yorkshire - although the loos are not quite what they expected

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The Hideout Hotel in Hull, run by Jodie Richardson and Enya Donohoe, is the final destination of the week. The hands-off check-in and self-catered apartments prove popular with some guests, but Barney kicks up a stink in the bathroom. During the DIY breakfast, Jodie's handling of a smoky situation sets alarm bells ringing for Carly and Lydia, and feedback leaves the hosts fired up with payment day looming

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On payment day, Carly from the Juniperlea Inn is keen for Barney to enlighten her on the reasons behind his sleep issues. Barney is in the spotlight again when his payment to Jodie and Enya makes tensions run high. There are plenty of differences to be debated before the winner is announced

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef again comes to the aid of failing American restaurants, starting in Fishtown, Philadelphia, where he tries to help the three owners of the Hot Potato Cafe, which has not made a profit in two years. Spurred on by a scathing review by an influential critic, he makes radical changes to alter the staff's attitudes and create food that is up to his own exacting standards

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The chef travels to Flamangos restaurant in Whitehouse, New Jersey, where he tries to help the mother-and-daughter owners turn around their struggling business's fortunes. He faces a struggle because parent Adelle has a bad relationship with her staff and is disappointed by the resident chef, leaving Gordon with the added challenge of changing her attitude

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay gives his advice to Paul Bazzini, an award-winning chef from New York who now owns a restaurant in Ridgewood, New Jersey. Paul confesses he has lost his passion for food and is struggling to keep up with the business, a situation that seems evident to other members of staff, his wife and customers

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton helps Marianne and Anthony from West Yorkshire search for their perfect holiday home in the Axarquia region of southern Spain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps a couple who run a pub-restaurant in County Durham to find a home on the Greek island of Kefalonia on a budget of £120,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps Leeds couple Neil Turner and Zoe Brown, who are planning to get married in Ibiza and whose wedding present to each other is to find a holiday home on the island

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin meets holistic therapist Daniela from East Yorkshire, who is looking to find a property on the coastline of western Madeira to live with her two rescue dogs. She wants a property with sea views, lots of outside space and the potential for a therapy room. With Daniela's budget of £100,000 and her mother Janet by her side to help, Jonnie Irwin lines up five options for her to choose between

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Ex-RAF officers Simon Stenning and Amanda Butcher have been visiting the Caribbean island of Antigua for several years, and now hope to find the perfect winter home there. Ben Hillman helps the pair, showing them five properties for their £330,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Elaine and Patrick Scott from Lancashire are both history buffs with a passion for the 1940s, and want to move permanently to Normandy in France. Drawn by rich culture and history, they're after a home in the Manche area. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties for their £140,000 budget

programme.title: The Windsors: Inside the Royal Dynasty
programme.desc: This episodem focuses on the early days of Elizabeth II's reign. The young queen takes her first steps as Queen, but finds herself torn between old tradition and those, like her husband, who want to modernise the monarchy

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a 17-year-old builder who has transformed a narrowboat into a floating holiday home, and a bride-to-be who has bought a disused burger van with the intention of turning it into a mobile bar for her wedding reception. George's Spanish tour continues with a visit to a snail-shaped house in the mountain valleys of Aragon

programme.title: 24 Hours in Police Custody
programme.desc: The police investigate an apparently unprovoked attack that has left a man fighting for his life after being shot in the chest at point blank range by a complete stranger. As Bedfordshire Police's Guns & Gangs unit comes under increasing pressure to catch the shooter before he strikes again, a woman arrives at Luton police station to report being threatened by a man who matches the description

programme.title: Queen Elizabeth and the Spy in the Palace
programme.desc: The story of Anthony Blunt, royal appointed art expert and KGB spy, who worked as Surveyor of the Queen's Pictures for 27 years. The programme examines how he was previously employed by George VI to loot art treasures from Germany in the aftermath of the Second World War, and that despite Blunt's protestations of loyalty to the monarchy, classified documents have since revealed that he provided damaging information about the royal family to his Soviet employers

programme.title: The Queen's Lost Family
programme.desc: Prince George makes a love match and marries the glamorous Princess Marina of Greece and Denmark, while Henry gives up the married Beryl Markham and also makes a suitable marriage. Edward, meanwhile, brings married American divorcee Wallis Simpson to George and Marina's pre-wedding party at Buckingham Palace, scandalising his parents. Not long after George V's Silver Jubilee celebrations, a unifying event for the nation, the King dies and Edward inherits the throne

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: This edition follows the stories of people who have lived through tragedy and life-changing circumstances, exploring how they've overcome their loss and adapted to the new paths their lives have taken. programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick treats Koa, a six-month-old Bouvier des Flandres who is suffering from a badly deformed back leg, causing huge pain. Patricia brings her beloved 11-year-old Rhodesian ridgeback, Kwanda, to Fitzpatrick's Guildford hospital with an eye infection that she fears may be the sign of something more sinister. Dr Laurent Findji confirms it is bad news - a malignant tumour

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Updates on stories featured in previous episodes, including Kent couple Rachel and Chris, who were pushed to the limit when their relationship with their tenant broke down and she stopped paying rent, leaving them more than £5,000 out of pocket. Plus, a young mum locked out of her home by the young couple she allowed to stay there for free while the wife had a baby, and a landlord who reduced her tenants' rent when they got into financial difficulties - only for the payments to end altogether

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Officers chase £4,500 in court fees in north Wales after a property deal turned sour, and in Birmingham the agents try to collect over £3,500 from a single mum who defaulted on loan repayments. Elsewhere in Worcestershire, the enforcement agents try to recover more than £10,000 from a shop-keeper for fraudulently keeping a deposit during a property sale

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Officers swoop on a housing estate searching for suspects involved in a shooting and it needs to be a tightly controlled operation and communication is key between the firearms unit, police helicopters and dog handler Duncan. Elsewhere, Steve responds to reports of a burglary in progress, and ends up on a chase through back gardens. After catching one of the suspects, Steve receives a shock when he returns to check out the burgled house

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Steve is involved in a dangerous high-speed pursuit of a car stolen in a burglary. The fleeing driver ditches and runs, but will the police chopper be able to keep him in their sights? Claire and Tom deal with an abusive drunk driver who is clueless about what he has done wrong, rookie Omar tackles a fence-hopping suspect whose antics have left him with a slashed hand, and Steve tracks down a car suspected of being involved in a robbery

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Charlie and Wayne are in hot pursuit of a van, but the driver crashes the vehicle and runs off into the night. With the help of a tracker, officers hunt down a 17-year-old suspected of committing a serious robbery, while Matt and Brimmers are on the hunt for the driver of a £300,000 Rolls-Royce who is thought to be under the influence

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers try to recover £5,500 owed by a travel agent to a dissatisfied customer in east London, and in Birmingham the agents chase over £2,000 owed by a convenience store owner to a supplier. In Staffordshire, two officers try to collect several thousand pounds owed by a pensioner after a dispute with his neighbour went all the way to the High Court

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!

programme.title: Filthy House SOS

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: In north London, Harrow council officers investigate an overcrowded property. An inspection uncovers many issues, including an unauthorised loft conversion that is a danger to the tenants who sleep there. A couple in Suffolk agree to rent a property to its former owner, who now refuses to leave, and a single mum tricks her way into a Milton Keynes property, confirming she has paid the deposit having done no such thing

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Housing officers in Harrow receive a tip-off about an overcrowded property and when they arrive they find 10 people living in dire conditions and a house that needs extensive emergency work. Squatters in Tottenham invade an elderly woman's home while she is away, ransacking the property and refusing to leave. In Waltham Abbey a seemingly perfect tenant forges her way into a desperate landlord's home, forcing him to move in with his parents while he struggles to evict his unruly occupant

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: The documentary about landlord and tenancy issues catches up on some of the stories featured so far. Emmanuel reveals what happened after he returned from a year working abroad to find himself £24,000 in mortgage arrears and unable to get into his own property. Andy made a mistake when he rented out his north London home to someone he knew, only for his so-called friend to sublet the property, pocket the profits and pay Andy nothing. What has happened since?

programme.title: Nightmare Tenants, Slum Landlords
programme.desc: Documentary highlighting alarming tales from all sides of the buy-to-let system, drawing attention to stories of unruly tenants who refuse to pay their dues and rogue landlords who have failed to live up to their responsibilities. The series also turns the spotlight on dishonest letting agents who have taken advantage of their position of trust to profit at the expense of both property owners and their tenants

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: High Court Enforcement agents evict a large group of squatters barricaded into a disused nursery in London, and have to resort to smashing their way in as they refuse to leave. Meanwhile, agents in Bristol try to recover over £10,000 owed by a plumbing business to a supplier, and emotions run high when they attempt to evict a tenant who pleads her innocence in west London

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: Stewart and Vic become party to a fractured family's school fee woes in Staffordshire and Del and Dael need to reclaim £3,500 after a rental van was returned badly damaged. Stewart and Elmor are in Warwickshire, where the members of a household blame their predicament on a scheming landlord whose subsequent arrival sparks a fierce reaction, while Iain helps Stewart remove a young mother from her Lancashire home

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The agents try to recover more than £12,000 owed by a jeweller to one of his suppliers, but as the tension mounts, the debtor violently lashes out at one of the officers. Meanwhile, agents in London chase up more than £5,000 owed by a tenant to her former landlord for rent arrears, and also attempt to retrieve over £6,000 owed by a property developer

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: In Staffordshire, the agents chase up a £3,000 deposit owed by a landlord to his tenant, and officers in Birmingham try to recover £25,000 from a nightclub owner for unpaid solicitor fees. Meanwhile, agents Max and Paul are in Surrey as they attempt to collect more than £3,000 owed to a garage after a customer disputed the repairs made to his van

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: The officers try to recover £5,500 owed by a travel agent to a dissatisfied customer in east London, and in Birmingham the agents chase over £2,000 owed by a convenience store owner to a supplier. In Staffordshire, two officers try to collect several thousand pounds owed by a pensioner after a dispute with his neighbour went all the way to the High Court

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Close friends Janet Perrie and Fiona Hope moved to the South Ayrshire village of Dipple in search of an idyllic new life for themselves and their 22 dogs. Unfortunately for them, other residents were enraged by the noise, smell and mess and wasted no time in contacting the authorities. In a cul-de-sac in Leicestershire, the Pollard, Walne and Elliott families have been harassed by 68-year-old Leslie Collins for more than a decade after they complained about the noise from an illegal wood-waste business he was running from his home. He then began to target them with increasingly intimidating behaviour - including mooning, two-finger salutes, shouting abuse, cutting holes in hedges and spying on them with binoculars

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: Documentary using eyewitness testimony and footage from camcorders, CCTV and phones to examine traumatic, shocking, humorous and occasionally bizarre feuds between neighbours. Including an Army veteran who was attacked with a knife, and a Polish couple forced to call the police after their neighbour threw petrol over one of them and threatened to burn down their conservatory

programme.title: Fifty Shades Darker (2017)
programme.desc: The bookish Anastasia Steele and damaged billionaire playboy Christian Grey continue their sado masochistic relationship, while their safety is threatened by strangers from his past. While Christian wrestles with his inner demons, Anastasia must confront the anger and envy of the women who came before her. Erotic drama sequel to Fifty Shades of Grey, starring Dakota Johnson, Jamie Dornan, Eric Johnson, Bella Heathcote and Rita Ora

Anthony Minghella's thriller, starring Matt Damon, Jude Law, Gwyneth Paltrow and Cate Blanchett

programme.desc: A young girl hires Jason to help her 'divorce' her parents ahead of Christmas, but mild amusement gives way to serious misgivings when a judge appoints him as her legal guardian

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy lieutenant is found dead in the Pentagon's custodial closet, and surveillance footage indicates she was killed by a co-worker. However, Ziva and the team discover her supposed assailant actually died of a heart attack days before the crime was committed. Meanwhile, suspicions are raised regarding the eyeball left in Tony's drink, and the investigators' attention is fixed on the port-to-port murderer, who appears to still be at large

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony's one-time Baltimore PD partner Danny Price is murdered in a manner that suggests the port-to-port killer has struck again. However, a key difference in the way the victim was bound suggests the crime was actually the work of a copycat, forcing the investigators to consider whether Price had any enemies

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Police officer Danny Pelham is murdered while staking out the apartment of a wanted man's girlfriend. The felon he was trying to snare becomes the chief suspect, and Briscoe and Curtis eventually track him down, only to discover the murdered cop had a history of violence against his girlfriend. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The team investigates when a respected journalist is shot shortly after writing a hard-hitting article on the New York drugs scene. It soon emerges that the story was fabricated and had earned the reporter his fair share of enemies, while an unexpected lead causes the officers to reopen a 20-year-old case concerning the murder of a famous actress. Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill star

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A hidden anti-Semitic message in a high-school yearbook offers an insight into the murder of an art teacher, and the subsequent case leads McCoy into conflict with 'Klan lawyer' Roy Payne

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The detectives are presented with a host of suspects while investigating the murder of a teenage drug user - and begin to suspect a member of her wealthy family could be responsible. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: The conviction of a death row prisoner is called into question by the revelation that attorney-client privilege may have prompted a former prosecutor to withhold evidence capable of establishing his innocence. Drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Steven Hill

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: A woman murdered while out jogging is found to be the spouse of a comedy club owner previously acquitted of killing his first wife. In order to secure a conviction, the team is forced to trace the part of a Colombian coin used in a subway. Award-winning police drama, starring Jerry Orbach and Benjamin Bratt

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New evidence reveals the serial killer has infiltrated the agency and is targeting a member of the NCIS team, prompting Gibbs to seek the guidance of his former mentor Mike Franks. A lead from Barrett's squad reveals the CIA has also been tracking the murderer - and its findings suggest the culprit could be an even deadlier foe than anticipated

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team attend the funeral of a fallen colleague before resuming their hunt for the man they believe to be the port-to-port killer. Vance frantically researches the suspect's background to learn more about his motivations and discover who his next target could be - but one of the investigators follows a clue that leads to danger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered and Gibbs and McGee are called in when she is discovered to be carrying ID bearing the name Sgt Patricia Moreno, a US marine known to have died several years earlier. The victim is discovered to have come to America from Afghanistan and a necklace reveals a fingerprint that leads the team to a known assassin. Guest starring Lolita Davidovich

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team's holiday plans are put on hold when a murder investigation leads to the discovery of a newborn baby who has no identification and no apparent ties to a family. Mark Harmon and Sean Murray star

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: A woman's murder bears the hallmarks of one committed by jailed serial killer Kevin Reddick, raising fears that he has found an accomplice to carry on his work while he is in prison. The investigation leads back to a series of experiments performed on Reddick to discover the cause of his madness

programme.title: Law & Order: Criminal Intent
programme.desc: The detectives investigate the discovery of a woman's body in the Bronx and learn she was reported missing 20 years previously. Suspicion falls on a businessman and his friend, but inquiries hit a dead end - until the team finds a link to two earlier rape cases with similarities to the murder

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Hawkes is persuaded by his girlfriend Camille to call in sick so he can join her at a food festival, but a stall explodes during the event - exposing his deception to his colleagues. The team's investigation reveals that something other than food was potentially being sold by the vendors, and a lead emerges with a link to another suspect

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: The detectives suspect a woman's murder could be the work of a serial killer, while Mac reconnects with his first partner, who is being stalked by an ex-con the pair put behind bars. Crime drama, guest starring Peter Fonda, with Gary Sinise

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote

programme.desc: Jessica helps a teenage tennis player whose desire to win is being ruined by her domineering father and a tragedy in her past, leading to some complex emotional problems. Angela Lansbury stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Journalist Jim Nash is murdered in the swamps of New Orleans, where voodoo is still a force to be reckoned with. Jessica risks its horrors and rituals to unmask the culprit. Angela Lansbury stars

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Two elderly people, both under the care of the same visiting nurse, die almost simultaneously and Jessica is asked to get to the bottom of the mystery. Angela Lansbury stars, with William Windom and Audra Lindley

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Part one of two. The sleuth also hears a tale about the possible illegal sale of ancient treasures. Guest starring Rod Taylor (The Birds), with Angela Lansbury

programme.desc: A doctor, arrested for sexually abusing his patients, is set free after Cassidy's testimony causes a mistrial. Lorenzo Scott and Dean Winters guest star

Detective drama, starring Vincent D'Onofrio and Jamey Sheridan

programme.title: Columbo: Murder - A Self Portrait (1989)
programme.desc: A possessive painter who fears his ex-wife will expose his dark secrets resorts to killing her, but makes it appear she drowned. However, Columbo is unconvinced and draws on his broad palette of experience to reveal the truth. Detective drama, starring Peter Falk, Patrick Bauchau and Fionnula Flanagan

programme.title: Columbo: Any Old Port in a Storm (1973)
programme.desc: A vineyard owner's financial security is jeopardised when his playboy younger brother threatens to sell the family winery. He resorts to killing his sibling and rigging the death scene to look like a scuba-diving accident. However, the crumpled cop sees through the ruse and in the process reveals he is something of a wine buff himself. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Donald Pleasence and Julie Harris

programme.title: Columbo: Ashes to Ashes (1998)
programme.desc: The shabby sleuth investigates the complex case of a murdered Hollywood gossip writer. He uses his unique talents to interrogate a smooth-talking funeral director, who was once romantically involved with the victim and one of the last people to see her alive. Detective drama, with Peter Falk. Patrick McGoohan co-stars and directs

programme.title: Columbo Likes the Nightlife (2003)
programme.desc: The disheveled cop is drawn into the world of the Los Angeles club scene when he investigates the apparent suicide of a tabloid reporter, and uncovers evidence of a blackmail plot. Detective drama, with Peter Falk, Matthew Rhys and Jennifer Sky

programme.title: Columbo: Caution - Murder Can Be Hazardous to Your Health (1991)
programme.desc: The host of a TV show is threatened by a rival, who has discovered evidence of his murky past. The presenter does away with his blackmailer using poisoned cigarettes, and seems to have pulled off the perfect crime - but a certain shabby sleuth is soon on the case and finds a few things that fail to add up. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk and George Hamilton

programme.title: Columbo: A Trace of Murder (1997)
programme.desc: A conniving woman yearns to divorce her husband and set up home with her lover - but a pre-nuptial agreement means she stands to lose out. Instead, she devises a cunning plan to nullify the contract, framing her unsuspecting spouse for the murder of a business rival. However, she reckons without the shabby sleuth's unfailing ability to uncover the truth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Shera Danese, Barry Corbin and David Rasche

programme.title: Columbo: An Exercise in Fatality (1974)
programme.desc: The owner of a chain of health clubs kills a business partner who was about to expose his crooked dealings. Making the death look like an unfortunate weightlifting accident, he cunningly creates the perfect alibi using a tape recording of his victim's voice - but he reckons without the intervention of the shabby sleuth. Crime drama, starring Peter Falk, Robert Conrad and Philip Bruns

programme.desc: Carisi opens a police investigation when his niece reports a classmate for sexual assault, and Stone learns there are no perfect witnesses when it comes to sex crimes

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Recently divorced Patricia Malty from Devon wants to make a fresh start on the Costa Calida in Spain after selling her home and moving in with her best friend, with Laura Hamilton helping her find suitable properties for her £90,000 budget

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Recently retired couple Nick and Jane Purser are looking forward to settling down, but cannot decide where that should be. Jane's mother lives in Liverpool, so she would prefer to stay close by with a move to Lancaster. Nick wants them to move to southern France, where he can ride his motorbike in the sun. Jasmine Harman shows the pair three properties in Lancaster for up to £180,000 and Jonnie Irwin tempts them with three properties for up to £145,000 in France

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: When her partner of 20 years died, Ginny Lunn decided to find a holiday home where she could create new memories. Torn between buying a place in Dorset within easy reach of her London home for a budget of £250,000, and the Costa Tropical in Spain, where she would have a budget of £150,000, she requires help. Jasmine Harman shows her three options in Dorset, and Laura Hamilton tries to impress her with three properties in Spain, but, with the help of her sister Sara, which one will Ginny choose?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton shows Juliette and Jonathan Cottam three homes in the Dordogne region of France, and Jasmine Harman tries to tempt them with three properties in Devon

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Property experts Jonnie Irwin and Jasmine Harman help a couple decide whether to relocate to Cornwall for an easy life, or move to Carcassonne in France, where they hope to find a spiritual retreat

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Ceri and Mike Green choose between the Lake District and the French Alps, with Jonnie Irwin showing them three UK properties and Laura Hamilton choosing a trio across the Channel

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Nick and Natalie Hammond are selling their family home in Hertfordshire and want to buy somewhere near the coast with more outdoor space for their four children. Nick's keen on a move to Mallorca, having holidayed in Spain as a child, but Natalie would prefer to move to Cornwall. They have a cash budget of £275,000 if they buy in Majorca where Laura Hamilton is on hand to help, or £375,000 if they buy in Cornwall, where Jonnie Irwin tries to beat Laura's properties

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Teenage sweethearts Laura and Stuart Queen have lived in the same house in Carlisle for 16 years but now want a change of location and lifestyle. Stuart wants to move to central Scotland, close to family and work. Horse-lover Laura thinks they'll get more property and land in Murcia in Spain. Jonnie Irwin tries to please them both with a budget of £150,000 in central Scotland and Laura Hamilton tempts them with properties in Spain for a budget of £120,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Chris and Sherrine Kingsley-Smith have spent most of their 14-year marriage travelling the world on a boat but are now ready to drop anchor for dry land. Chris is keen on the rural lifestyle of the Cotswolds, where they have friends, but Sherrine has her heart set on the Dordogne region of France, where they're currently renting. Jonnie Irwin shows them places in the UK for their budget of £700,000, while Jasmine Harman tempts them with some amazing properties in France for up to £900,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Syd and Caroline Keenan from Cheshire want to buy a holiday home for their family but can't decide whether it should be in Wales's Llyn Peninsula, where Caroline has strong family connections, or Sesimbra in Portugal: a place Syd loves for its culture, cuisine and climate. programme.desc: A group of heroes try to stop aliens taking over the universe

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls take on the Mixed Tapes on a game show, but their rivals cheat by exploiting their worst fears

programme.title: K3
programme.desc: The girls are tricked into appearing on a reality show in the Himalayas, only to find the director has set up booby traps all over the mountains to make things more interesting

programme.title: Heidi
programme.desc: Follow Heidi as she discovers life's ups and downs. She lights up and soothes the people and animals all around her, with her enthusiastic, generous and wonderful zest for life

programme.title: Kong: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Kong, Jason, jungle girl Lua, fun-loving Tann and wise Dr. Jenkins take on power-hungry scientists, ferocious prehistoric creatures and incredible magical beings to protect their unique island home

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Gilby challenges Vic to prove his courage by spending the night alone in a forest full of hungry wolves

programme.title: Vic the Viking
programme.desc: Halvar is challenged to a race by a haughty rival captain with a spectacular boat - but Vic hatches a plan to give the Drakkar an extra burst of speed

programme.title: Dork Hunters from Outer Space
programme.desc: A group of heroes try to stop aliens taking over the universe

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The Paris-based chef returns to her home city to see what its most interesting eateries have to offer. In the first edition, she visits a pop-up restaurant in a boutique

programme.title: Rachel Khoo's Kitchen Notebook
programme.desc: The chef creates simple and colourful canapes, before making a sophisticated jelly for grown-ups

programme.title: Greatest Miracles Easter Concert: The Great Miracle
programme.desc: The Tabernacle Choir sing classical greats in this series of special Easter performances of music and spoken word

programme.title: Greatest Miracles Easter Concert: Light of Hope
programme.desc: The Tabernacle Choir sing classical greats in this series of special Easter performances of music and spoken word

programme.title: The Knowledge: The World's Toughest Taxi Test
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at the test that all black cab drivers working in the city of London must take, one of the world's toughest exams - The Knowledge of London. Each candidate must memorise the capital's 25,000 streets, traffic restrictions and ever-changing points of interest, which takes an average of four years of determination to successfully pass

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Best of The Tube
programme.desc: Recapping the finest moments from the music show that was hosted by Jools Holland and the late Paula Yates

programme.title: Manganinnie (1980)
programme.desc: An Australian Aboriginal woman goes in search of her lost tribe, accompanied on her journey by a lost white girl. with whom she forms a close bond despite having no common language. As their journey towards the coast continues, the woman begins to suspect her people and culture may have been all but wiped out by British colonists. Historical drama, starring Mawuyul Yanthalawuy and Anna Ralph

programme.title: We Dive at Dawn (1943)
programme.desc: A British submarine crew is unexpectedly ordered to carry out a top-secret mission to sink the German fleet's latest battleship in the Baltic Sea. But it is not long before disaster strikes and the vessel becomes stranded in the middle of the ocean with no fuel or food. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Eric Portman, Reginald Purdell and Niall MacGinnis

programme.title: Bloomfield (1970)
programme.desc: An Israeli footballer nearing the end of his career is tempted to accept a bribe to throw his final game. A devoted young fan and a woman who is in love with him prick his conscience, but even if he chooses to do the right thing, his failing skills may not be enough to triumph over younger, fitter opposition. Sporting drama based on Joseph Gross' novel, co-directed by and starring Richard Harris, with Romy Schneider and Kim Burfield

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours narrated by Dewyne Lindsay, this time exploring Canary Wharf and Southbank.

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh, a student at Kew in the 1970s, offers an insight into the work carried out behind the scenes by the 150 gardening staff at the Royal Botanic Gardens. Palm House keeper Emma Fox is seen doing battle with a plague of thrips, while tree gang member Jon Hammerton fells a diseased tree

programme.title: A Year at Kew
programme.desc: Expert in foul odours Dr Geoffrey Kite eagerly anticipates the rare flowering of the mighty Titan arum, an evil-smelling bloom lasting just 24 hours. Simon and Tim Cole work against the clock to complete the badger sett and field hospital in time for Kew's Go Wild festival

programme.title: My Greek Odyssey
programme.desc: Peter Maneas embarks on a journey through some of the most spectacular and unique Greek islands, exploring the rich culture, history and food of Greece while meeting the locals and those who live there

programme.title: Queens
programme.desc: 16th-century Europe was a hotbed of tense international intrigue. Mary Stuart and Elizabeth I confront one another on matters religious, political and personal

programme.title: The Hatton Garden Job (2017)
programme.desc: A ragtag group of career criminals plot an audacious, multi-million-pound raid on an underground facility in London. True-life crime drama, with Stephen Moyer, Larry Lamb and Matthew Goode

programme.title: Operation Chromite (2016)
programme.desc: A squad of South Korean soldiers sent to infiltrate their northern neighbours during the crucial Battle of Incheon. Wartime drama, starring Lee Jung-jae and Liam Neeson

programme.title: Eugenie De Sade (1973)
programme.desc: A teenage girl lives alone with her stepfather after the death of her mother, and becomes fascinated with the dark erotic stories he writes. As she grows more and more sexually obsessed with him and his tales, she fails to realise the dangerous secrets that have inspired his creations. Erotic thriller, starring Soledad Miranda and Paul Muller

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick decides to dedicate his life to religion, while Joe receives decorating advice from Roger Moore. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Walks Around Britain
programme.desc: Andrew White walks from Banavie to Gairlocky and around the Ribblehead Viaduct

programme.desc: The Tabernacle Choir sing classical greats in this series of special Easter performances of music and spoken word

programme.title: Skid Kids (1953)
programme.desc: A group of kids takes on a gang of bike thieves, with a little help from the police. Children's drama, starring Barry MacGregor and Anthony Lang

programme.title: Henry V (1989)
programme.desc: The English king leads his armies into war to reclaim control of France - culminating in a pivotal confrontation with much larger enemy forces on the fields of Agincourt. Adaptation of Shakespeare's historical play, directed by and starring Kenneth Branagh. With Derek Jacobi, Brian Blessed, Emma Thompson, Ian Holm, Robert Stephens and a young Christian Bale

programme.title: The Likely Lads (1976)
programme.desc: Bob and Thelma embark on a chaotic caravan holiday - with best pal Terry and a glamorous Scandinavian au pair in tow, ensuring that nothing will go according to plan. Comedy based on the TV series, starring James Bolam, Rodney Bewes, Brigit Forsyth and Mary Tamm

programme.title: One Good Turn (1954)
programme.desc: A boy living in an orphanage grows up to be the institution's lovable but clumsy caretaker. He must save the children's home from a wicked property developer who wants to build a factory on the site, and tricks him into taking the kids to the seaside so he can knock the building down. Comedy, starring Norman Wisdom and Joan Rice

programme.title: The Fast Lady (1963)
programme.desc: A cycling enthusiast on a modest civil servant's salary gives up his beloved bike to a buy a luxury car to impress a local magistrate's daughter. However, he first has to pass his driving test to stand any chance of winning her heart - and in the process ends up in a wild car-chase after a gang of robbers. Comedy, with Julie Christie, Stanley Baxter, James Robertson Justice, Leslie Phillips and Dick Emery

programme.title: London Districts
programme.desc: Short visual tours exploring Forest Hill, Hither Green and Cubitt Town. Narrated by Dewyne Lindsay

programme.title: Killing Hitler
programme.desc: Part one of two. A blend of documentary, dramatic reconstruction and expert military analysis brings to life the blueprint of Operation Foxley - a 1944 plan to assassinate Adolf Hitler. Featuring testimonies from operatives of Churchill's Special Operations Executive and one of the Fuehrer's most trusted bodyguards, the ethical right to kill an enemy head of state is debated during the unfolding story of LB/X - the man in charge of the plot to kill the Allies' most hated foe

programme.title: The Comic Strip Presents
programme.desc: Two inept hitmen pursue a banker who has embezzled from the Mafia through the streets of London - but have no clue what to do with him when they catch him

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick decides to dedicate his life to religion, while Joe receives decorating advice from Roger Moore. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: Escape from Sobibor (1987)
programme.desc: New arrivals at a Nazi concentration camp realise they do not have long to live as the Nazi campaign of genocide gathers speed. The commandant threatens to execute one prisoner for every escape attempt - leading the inmates to realise that the only hope is for all 600 of them to break out together. Drama based on the true story of the largest uprising during the Holocaust, starring Alan Arkin, Joanna Pacula and Rutger Hauer

programme.title: Urban Hymn (2015)
programme.desc: A teenager is encouraged by her social worker to use singing as an escape route from her troubled personal life. She soon finds her loyalties divided between the fresh start music provides for her and the influence of her possessive and volatile best friend. Drama, starring Letitia Wright, Shirley Henderson and Ian Hart

programme.title: Stella Street
programme.desc: Mick's preaching starts to annoy the neighbours and Dirk Bogarde reveals some information to the police. Comedy, written and performed by John Sessions and Phil Cornwell

programme.title: A London Community in Lockdown
programme.desc: A heartfelt look at life during lockdown in the Listraban community in London, a record of a moment in time that will be in the history books for generations to come
